This can be ``False`` if the\\n109:         estimator supports only multi-output cases.\\n110:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n111: \\n112:     required: bool\\n113:     one_d_labels: bool = False\\n114:     two_d_labels: bool = False\\n115:     positive_only: bool = False\\n116:     multi_output: bool = False\\n117:     single_output: bool = True\\n118: \\n119: \\n120: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\\n121: class TransformerTags:\\n122:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Tags for the transformer.\\n123: \\n124:     Parameters\\n125:     ----------\\n126:     preserves_dtype : list[str], default=[\\\"float64\\\"]\\n127:         Applies only on transformers. It corresponds to the data types\\n128:         which will be preserved such that `X_trans.dtype` is the same\\n129:         as `X.dtype` after calling `transformer.transform(X)`. If this\\n130:         list is empty, then the transformer is not expected to\\n131:         preserve the data type. The first value in the list is\\n132:         considered as the default data type, corresponding to the data\\n133:         type of the output when the input data type is not going to be\\n134:         preserved.\\n135:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n136: \\n137:     preserves_dtype: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: [\\\"float64\\\"])\\n138: \\n139: \\n140: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\\n141: class ClassifierTags:\\n142:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Tags for the classifier.\\n143: \\n144:     Parameters\\n145:     ----------\\n146:     poor_score : bool, default=False\\n147:         Whether the estimator fails to provide a \\\"reasonable\\\" test-set\\n148:         score, which currently for classification is an accuracy of\\n149:         0.83 on ``make_blobs(n_samples=300, random_state=0)``. The\\n150:         datasets and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\\n151:         and might be replaced by something more systematic.\\n152: \\n153:     multi_class : bool, default=True\\n154:         Whether the classifier can handle multi-class\\n155:         classification. Note that all classifiers support binary\\n156:         classification. Therefore this flag indicates whether the\\n157:         classifier is a binary-classifier-only or not.\\n158: \\n159:         See :term:`multi-class` in the glossary.\\n160: \\n161:     multi_label : bool, default=False\\n162:         Whether the classifier supports multi-label output: a data point can\\n163:         be predicted to belong to a variable number of classes.\\n164: \\n165:         See :term:`multi-label` in the glossary.\\n166:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n167: \\n168:     poor_score: bool = False\\n169:     multi_class: bool = True\\n170:     multi_label: bool = False\\n171: \\n172: \\n173: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\\n174: class RegressorTags:\\n175:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Tags for the regressor.\\n176: \\n177:     Parameters\\n178:     ----------\\n179:     poor_score : bool, default=False\\n180:         Whether the estimator fails to provide a \\\"reasonable\\\" test-set\\n181:         score, which currently for regression is an R2 of 0.5 on\\n182:         ``make_regression(n_samples=200, n_features=10,\\n183:         n_informative=1, bias=5.0, noise=20, random_state=42)``. The\\n184:         dataset and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\\n185:         and might be replaced by something more systematic.\\n186:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n187: \\n188:     poor_score: bool = False\\n189: \\n190: \\n191: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\\n192: class Tags:\\n193:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Tags for the estimator.\\n194: \\n195:     See :ref:`estimator_tags` for more information.\\n196: \\n197:     Parameters\\n198:     ----------\\n199:     estimator_type : str or None\\n200:         The type of the estimator. Can be one of:\\n201:         - \\\"classifier\\\"\\n202:         - \\\"regressor\\\"\\n203:         - \\\"transformer\\\"\\n204:         - \\\"clusterer\\\"\\n205:         - \\\"outlier_detector\\\"\\n206:         - \\\"density_estimator\\\"\\n207: \\n208:     target_tags : :class:`TargetTags`\\n209:         The target(y) tags.\\n210: \\n211:     transformer_tags : :class:`TransformerTags` or None\\n212:         The transformer tags.\\n213: \\n214:     classifier_tags : :class:`ClassifierTags` or None\\n215:         The classifier tags.\\n216: \\n217:     regressor_tags : :class:`RegressorTags` or None\\n218:         The regressor tags.\\n219: \\n220:     array_api_support : bool, default=False\\n221:         Whether the estimator supports Array API compatible inputs.\\n222: \\n223:     no_validation : bool, default=False\\n224:         Whether the estimator skips input-validation. This is only meant for\\n225:         stateless and dummy transformers!\\n226: \\n227:     non_deterministic : bool, default=False\\n228:         Whether the estimator is not deterministic given a fixed ``random_state``.\\n229: \\n230:     requires_fit : bool, default=True\\n231:         Whether the estimator requires to be fitted before calling one of\\n232:         `transform`, `predict`, `predict_proba`, or `decision_function`.\\n233: \\n234:     _skip_test : bool, default=False\\n235:         Whether to skip common tests entirely. Don't use this unless\\n236:         you have a *very good* reason.\\n237: \\n238:     input_tags : :class:`InputTags`\\n239:         The input data(X) tags.\\n240:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n241: \\n242:     estimator_type: str | None\\n243:     target_tags: TargetTags\\n244:     transformer_tags: TransformerTags | None = None\\n245:     classifier_tags: ClassifierTags | None = None\\n246:     regressor_tags: RegressorTags | None = None\\n247:     array_api_support: bool = False\\n248:     no_validation: bool = False\\n249:     non_deterministic: bool = False\\n250:     requires_fit: bool = True\\n251:     _skip_test: bool = False\\n252:     input_tags: InputTags = field(default_factory=InputTags)\\n253: \\n254: \\n255: # TODO(1.8): Remove this function\\n256: def default_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\\n257:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the default tags for an estimator.\\n258: \\n259:     This ignores any ``__sklearn_tags__`` method that the estimator may have.\\n260: \\n261:     If the estimator is a classifier or a regressor, ``target_tags.required``\\n262:     will be set to ``True``, otherwise it will be set to ``False``.\\n263: \\n264:     ``transformer_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils. TransformerTags` if the\\n265:     estimator has a ``transform`` or ``fit_transform`` method, otherwise it will be set\\n266:     to ``None``.\\n267: \\n268:     ``classifier_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.ClassifierTags` if the\\n269:     estimator is a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\\n270:     a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\\n271: \\n272:     ``regressor_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.RegressorTags` if the\\n273:     estimator is a regressor, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\\n274: \\n275:     Parameters\\n276:     ----------\\n277:     estimator : estimator object\\n278:         The estimator for which to get the default tags.\\n279: \\n280:     Returns\\n281:     -------\\n282:     tags : Tags\\n283:         The default tags for the estimator.\\n284:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n285:     est_is_classifier = getattr(estimator, \\\"_estimator_type\\\", None) == \\\"classifier\\\"\\n286:     est_is_regressor = getattr(estimator, \\\"_estimator_type\\\", None) == \\\"regressor\\\"\\n287:     target_required = est_is_classifier or est_is_regressor\\n288: \\n289:     return Tags(\\n290:         estimator_type=getattr(estimator, \\\"_estimator_type\\\", None),\\n291:         target_tags=TargetTags(required=target_required),\\n292:         transformer_tags=(\\n293:             TransformerTags()\\n294:             if hasattr(estimator, \\\"transform\\\") or hasattr(estimator, \\\"fit_transform\\\")\\n295:             else None\\n296:         ),\\n297:         classifier_tags=ClassifierTags() if est_is_classifier else None,\\n298:         regressor_tags=RegressorTags() if est_is_regressor else None,\\n299:     )\\n300: \\n301: \\n302: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\\n303: def _find_tags_provider(estimator, warn=True):\\n304:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Find the tags provider for an estimator.\\n305: \\n306:     Parameters\\n307:     ----------\\n308:     estimator : estimator object\\n309:         The estimator to find the tags provider for.\\n310: \\n311:     warn : bool, default=True\\n312:         Whether to warn if the tags provider is not found.\\n313: \\n314:     Returns\\n315:     -------\\n316:     tag_provider : str\\n317:         The tags provider for the estimator. Can be one of:\\n318:         - \\\"_get_tags\\\": to use the old tags infrastructure\\n319:         - \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\": to use the new tags infrastructure\\n320:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n321:     mro_model = type(estimator).mro()\\n322:     tags_mro = OrderedDict()\\n323:     for klass in mro_model:\\n324:         tags_provider = []\\n325:         if \\\"_more_tags\\\" in vars(klass):\\n326:             tags_provider.append(\\\"_more_tags\\\")\\n327:         if \\\"_get_tags\\\" in vars(klass):\\n328:             tags_provider.append(\\\"_get_tags\\\")\\n329:         if \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\" in vars(klass):\\n330:             tags_provider.append(\\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\")\\n331:         tags_mro[klass.__name__] = tags_provider\\n332: \\n333:     all_providers = set(chain.from_iterable(tags_mro.values()))\\n334:     if \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\" not in all_providers:\\n335:         # default on the old tags infrastructure\\n336:         return \\\"_get_tags\\\"\\n337: \\n338:     tag_provider = \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\"\\n339:     for klass in tags_mro:\\n340:         has_get_or_more_tags = any(\\n341:             provider in tags_mro[klass] for provider in (\\\"_get_tags\\\", \\\"_more_tags\\\")\\n342:         )\\n343:         has_sklearn_tags = \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\" in tags_mro[klass]\\n344: \\n345:         if tags_mro[klass] and tag_provider == \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\":  # is it empty\\n346:             if has_get_or_more_tags and not has_sklearn_tags:\\n347:                 # Case where a class does not implement __sklearn_tags__ and we fallback\\n348:                 # to _get_tags. We should therefore warn for implementing\\n349:                 # __sklearn_tags__.\\n350:                 tag_provider = \\\"_get_tags\\\"\\n351:                 break\\n352: \\n353:     if warn and tag_provider == \\\"_get_tags\\\":\\n354:         warnings.warn(\\n355:             f\\\"The {estimator.__class__.__name__} or classes from which it inherits \\\"\\n356:             \\\"use `_get_tags` and `_more_tags`. Please define the \\\"\\n357:             \\\"`__sklearn_tags__` method, or inherit from `sklearn.base.BaseEstimator` \\\"\\n358:             \\\"and/or other appropriate mixins such as `sklearn.base.TransformerMixin`, \\\"\\n359:             \\\"`sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`, `sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`, and \\\"\\n360:             \\\"`sklearn.base.OutlierMixin`. From scikit-learn 1.7, not defining \\\"\\n361:             \\\"`__sklearn_tags__` will raise an error.\\\",\\n362:             category=DeprecationWarning,\\n363:         )\\n364:     return tag_provider\\n365: \\n366: \\n367: def get_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\\n368:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Get estimator tags.\\n369: \\n370:     :class:`~sklearn.BaseEstimator` provides the estimator tags machinery.\\n371:     However, if an estimator does not inherit from this base class, we should\\n372:     fall-back to the default tags.\\n373: \\n374:     For scikit-learn built-in estimators, we should still rely on\\n375:     `self.__sklearn_tags__()`. `get_tags(est)` should be used when we\\n376:     are not sure where `est` comes from: typically\\n377:     `get_tags(self.estimator)` where `self` is a meta-estimator, or in\\n378:     the common checks.\\n379: \\n380:     .. versionadded:: 1.6\\n381: \\n382:     Parameters\\n383:     ----------\\n384:     estimator : estimator object\\n385:         The estimator from which to get the tag.\\n386: \\n387:     Returns\\n388:     -------\\n389:     tags : :class:`~.sklearn.utils.Tags`\\n390:         The estimator tags.\\n391:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n392: \\n393:     tag_provider = _find_tags_provider(estimator)\\n394: \\n395:     if tag_provider == \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\":\\n396:         # TODO(1.7): turn the warning into an error\\n397:         try:\\n398:             tags = estimator.__sklearn_tags__()\\n399:         except AttributeError as exc:\\n400:             if str(exc) == \\\"'super' object has no attribute '__sklearn_tags__'\\\":\\n401:                 # workaround the regression reported in\\n402:                 # https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\\n403:                 # `__sklearn_tags__` is implemented by calling\\n404:                 # `super().__sklearn_tags__()` but there is no `__sklearn_tags__`\\n405:                 # method in the base class.\\n406:                 warnings.warn(\\n407:                     f\\\"The following error was raised: {str(exc)}. It seems that \\\"\\n408:                     \\\"there are no classes that implement `__sklearn_tags__` \\\"\\n409:                     \\\"in the MRO and/or all classes in the MRO call \\\"\\n410:                     \\\"`super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Make sure to inherit from \\\"\\n411:                     \\\"`BaseEstimator` which implements `__sklearn_tags__` (or \\\"\\n412:                     \\\"alternatively define `__sklearn_tags__` but we don't recommend \\\"\\n413:                     \\\"this approach). Note that `BaseEstimator` needs to be on the \\\"\\n414:                     \\\"right side of other Mixins in the inheritance order. The \\\"\\n415:                     \\\"default are now used instead since retrieving tags failed. \\\"\\n416:                     \\\"This warning will be replaced by an error in 1.7.\\\",\\n417:                     category=DeprecationWarning,\\n418:                 )\\n419:                 tags = default_tags(estimator)\\n420:             else:\\n421:                 raise\\n422:     else:\\n423:         # TODO(1.7): Remove this branch of the code\\n424:         # Let's go through the MRO and patch each class implementing _more_tags\\n425:         sklearn_tags_provider = {}\\n426:         more_tags_provider = {}\\n427:         class_order = []\\n428:         for klass in reversed(type(estimator).mro()):\\n429:             if \\\"__sklearn_tags__\\\" in vars(klass):\\n430:                 sklearn_tags_provider[klass] = klass.__sklearn_tags__(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n431:                 class_order.append(klass)\\n432:             elif \\\"_more_tags\\\" in vars(klass):\\n433:                 more_tags_provider[klass] = klass._more_tags(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n434:                 class_order.append(klass)\\n435: \\n436:         # Find differences between consecutive in the case of __sklearn_tags__\\n437:         # inheritance\\n438:         sklearn_tags_diff = {}\\n439:         items = list(sklearn_tags_provider.items())\\n440:         for current_item, next_item in zip(items[:-1], items[1:]):\\n441:             current_name, current_tags = current_item\\n442:             next_name, next_tags = next_item\\n443:             current_tags = _to_old_tags(current_tags)\\n444:             next_tags = _to_old_tags(next_tags)\\n445: \\n446:             # Compare tags and store differences\\n447:             diff = {}\\n448:             for key in current_tags:\\n449:                 if current_tags[key] != next_tags[key]:\\n450:                     diff[key] = next_tags[key]\\n451: \\n452:             sklearn_tags_diff[next_name] = diff\\n453: \\n454:         tags = {}\\n455:         for klass in class_order:\\n456:             if klass in sklearn_tags_diff:\\n457:                 tags.update(sklearn_tags_diff[klass])\\n458:             elif klass in more_tags_provider:\\n459:                 tags.update(more_tags_provider[klass])\\n460: \\n461:         tags = _to_new_tags(\\n462:             {**_to_old_tags(default_tags(estimator)), **tags}, estimator\\n463:         )\\n464: \\n465:     return tags\\n466: \\n467: \\n468: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\\n469: def _safe_tags(estimator, key=None):\\n470:     warnings.warn(\\n471:         \\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed in \\\"\\n472:         \\\"1.7. Use the public `get_tags` function instead and make sure to implement \\\"\\n473:         \\\"the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\\\",\\n474:         category=DeprecationWarning,\\n475:     )\\n476:     tags = _to_old_tags(get_tags(estimator))\\n477: \\n478:     if key is not None:\\n479:         if key not in tags:\\n480:             raise ValueError(\\n481:                 f\\\"The key {key} is not defined for the class \\\"\\n482:                 f\\\"{estimator.__class__.__name__}.\\\"\\n483:             )\\n484:         return tags[key]\\n485:     return tags\\n486: \\n487: \\n488: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\\n489: def _to_new_tags(old_tags, estimator=None):\\n490:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n491:     input_tags = InputTags(\\n492:         one_d_array=\\\"1darray\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n493:         two_d_array=\\\"2darray\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n494:         three_d_array=\\\"3darray\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n495:         sparse=\\\"sparse\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n496:         categorical=\\\"categorical\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n497:         string=\\\"string\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n498:         dict=\\\"dict\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n499:         positive_only=old_tags[\\\"requires_positive_X\\\"],\\n500:         allow_nan=old_tags[\\\"allow_nan\\\"],\\n501:         pairwise=old_tags[\\\"pairwise\\\"],\\n502:     )\\n503:     target_tags = TargetTags(\\n504:         required=old_tags[\\\"requires_y\\\"],\\n505:         one_d_labels=\\\"1dlabels\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n506:         two_d_labels=\\\"2dlabels\\\" in old_tags[\\\"X_types\\\"],\\n507:         positive_only=old_tags[\\\"requires_positive_y\\\"],\\n508:         multi_output=old_tags[\\\"multioutput\\\"] or old_tags[\\\"multioutput_only\\\"],\\n509:         single_output=not old_tags[\\\"multioutput_only\\\"],\\n510:     )\\n511:     if estimator is not None and (\\n512:         hasattr(estimator, \\\"transform\\\") or hasattr(estimator, \\\"fit_transform\\\")\\n513:     ):\\n514:         transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\\n515:             preserves_dtype=old_tags[\\\"preserves_dtype\\\"],\\n516:         )\\n517:     else:\\n518:         transformer_tags = None\\n519:     estimator_type = getattr(estimator, \\\"_estimator_type\\\", None)\\n520:     if estimator_type == \\\"classifier\\\":\\n521:         classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\\n522:             poor_score=old_tags[\\\"poor_score\\\"],\\n523:             multi_class=not old_tags[\\\"binary_only\\\"],\\n524:             multi_label=old_tags[\\\"multilabel\\\"],\\n525:         )\\n526:     else:\\n527:         classifier_tags = None\\n528:     if estimator_type == \\\"regressor\\\":\\n529:         regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\\n530:             poor_score=old_tags[\\\"poor_score\\\"],\\n531:         )\\n532:     else:\\n533:         regressor_tags = None\\n534:     return Tags(\\n535:         estimator_type=estimator_type,\\n536:         target_tags=target_tags,\\n537:         transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\\n538:         classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\\n539:         regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\\n540:         input_tags=input_tags,\\n541:         array_api_support=old_tags[\\\"array_api_support\\\"],\\n542:         no_validation=old_tags[\\\"no_validation\\\"],\\n543:         non_deterministic=old_tags[\\\"non_deterministic\\\"],\\n544:         requires_fit=old_tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"],\\n545:         _skip_test=old_tags[\\\"_skip_test\\\"],\\n546:     )\\n547: \\n548: \\n549: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\\n550: def _to_old_tags(new_tags):\\n551:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n552:     if new_tags.classifier_tags:\\n553:         binary_only = not new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_class\\n554:         multilabel = new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_label\\n555:         poor_score_clf = new_tags.classifier_tags.poor_score\\n556:     else:\\n557:         binary_only = False\\n558:         multilabel = False\\n559:         poor_score_clf = False\\n560: \\n561:     if new_tags.regressor_tags:\\n562:         poor_score_reg = new_tags.regressor_tags.poor_score\\n563:     else:\\n564:         poor_score_reg = False\\n565: \\n566:     if new_tags.transformer_tags:\\n567:         preserves_dtype = new_tags.transformer_tags.preserves_dtype\\n568:     else:\\n569:         preserves_dtype = [\\\"float64\\\"]\\n570: \\n571:     tags = {\\n572:         \\\"allow_nan\\\": new_tags.input_tags.allow_nan,\\n573:         \\\"array_api_support\\\": new_tags.array_api_support,\\n574:         \\\"binary_only\\\": binary_only,\\n575:         \\\"multilabel\\\": multilabel,\\n576:         \\\"multioutput\\\": new_tags.target_tags.multi_output,\\n577:         \\\"multioutput_only\\\": (\\n578:             not new_tags.target_tags.single_output and new_tags.target_tags.multi_output\\n579:         ),\\n580:         \\\"no_validation\\\": new_tags.no_validation,\\n581:         \\\"non_deterministic\\\": new_tags.non_deterministic,\\n582:         \\\"pairwise\\\": new_tags.input_tags.pairwise,\\n583:         \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": preserves_dtype,\\n584:         \\\"poor_score\\\": poor_score_clf or poor_score_reg,\\n585:         \\\"requires_fit\\\": new_tags.requires_fit,\\n586:         \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": new_tags.input_tags.positive_only,\\n587:         \\\"requires_y\\\": new_tags.target_tags.required,\\n588:         \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": new_tags.target_tags.positive_only,\\n589:         \\\"_skip_test\\\": new_tags._skip_test,\\n590:         \\\"stateless\\\": new_tags.requires_fit,\\n591:     }\\n592:     X_types = []\\n593:     if new_tags.input_tags.one_d_array:\\n594:         X_types.append(\\\"1darray\\\")\\n595:     if new_tags.input_tags.two_d_array:\\n596:         X_types.append(\\\"2darray\\\")\\n597:     if new_tags.input_tags.three_d_array:\\n598:         X_types.append(\\\"3darray\\\")\\n599:     if new_tags.input_tags.sparse:\\n600:         X_types.append(\\\"sparse\\\")\\n601:     if new_tags.input_tags.categorical:\\n602:         X_types.append(\\\"categorical\\\")\\n603:     if new_tags.input_tags.string:\\n604:         X_types.append(\\\"string\\\")\\n605:     if new_tags.input_tags.dict:\\n606:         X_types.append(\\\"dict\\\")\\n607:     if new_tags.target_tags.one_d_labels:\\n608:         X_types.append(\\\"1dlabels\\\")\\n609:     if new_tags.target_tags.two_d_labels:\\n610:         X_types.append(\\\"2dlabels\\\")\\n611:     tags[\\\"X_types\\\"] = X_types\\n612:     return tags\\n613: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/sklearn/utils/_tags.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: from __future__ import annotations\n2: \n3: import warnings\n4: from collections import OrderedDict\n5: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n6: from itertools import chain\n7: \n8: from .fixes import _dataclass_args\n9: \n10: # Authors: The scikit-learn developers\n11: # SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause\n12: \n13: \n14: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\n15: class InputTags:\n16:     \"\"\"Tags for the input data.\n17: \n18:     Parameters\n19:     ----------\n20:     one_d_array : bool, default=False\n21:         Whether the input can be a 1D array.\n22: \n23:     two_d_array : bool, default=True\n24:         Whether the input can be a 2D array. Note that most common\n25:         tests currently run only if this flag is set to ``True``.\n26: \n27:     three_d_array : bool, default=False\n28:         Whether the input can be a 3D array.\n29: \n30:     sparse : bool, default=False\n31:         Whether the input can be a sparse matrix.\n32: \n33:     categorical : bool, default=False\n34:         Whether the input can be categorical.\n35: \n36:     string : bool, default=False\n37:         Whether the input can be an array-like of strings.\n38: \n39:     dict : bool, default=False\n40:         Whether the input can be a dictionary.\n41: \n42:     positive_only : bool, default=False\n43:         Whether the estimator requires positive X.\n44: \n45:     allow_nan : bool, default=False\n46:         Whether the estimator supports data with missing values encoded as `np.nan`.\n47: \n48:     pairwise : bool, default=False\n49:         This boolean attribute indicates whether the data (`X`),\n50:         :term:`fit` and similar methods consists of pairwise measures\n51:         over samples rather than a feature representation for each\n52:         sample.  It is usually `True` where an estimator has a\n53:         `metric` or `affinity` or `kernel` parameter with value\n54:         'precomputed'. Its primary purpose is to support a\n55:         :term:`meta-estimator` or a cross validation procedure that\n56:         extracts a sub-sample of data intended for a pairwise\n57:         estimator, where the data needs to be indexed on both axes.\n58:         Specifically, this tag is used by\n59:         `sklearn.utils.metaestimators._safe_split` to slice rows and\n60:         columns.\n61: \n62:         Note that if setting this tag to ``True`` means the estimator can take only\n63:         positive values, the `positive_only` tag must reflect it and also be set to\n64:         ``True``.\n65:     \"\"\"\n66: \n67:     one_d_array: bool = False\n68:     two_d_array: bool = True\n69:     three_d_array: bool = False\n70:     sparse: bool = False\n71:     categorical: bool = False\n72:     string: bool = False\n73:     dict: bool = False\n74:     positive_only: bool = False\n75:     allow_nan: bool = False\n76:     pairwise: bool = False\n77: \n78: \n79: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\n80: class TargetTags:\n81:     \"\"\"Tags for the target data.\n82: \n83:     Parameters\n84:     ----------\n85:     required : bool\n86:         Whether the estimator requires y to be passed to `fit`,\n87:         `fit_predict` or `fit_transform` methods. The tag is ``True``\n88:         for estimators inheriting from `~sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`\n89:         and `~sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`.\n90: \n91:     one_d_labels : bool, default=False\n92:         Whether the input is a 1D labels (y).\n93: \n94:     two_d_labels : bool, default=False\n95:         Whether the input is a 2D labels (y).\n96: \n97:     positive_only : bool, default=False\n98:         Whether the estimator requires a positive y (only applicable\n99:         for regression).\n100: \n101:     multi_output : bool, default=False\n102:         Whether a regressor supports multi-target outputs or a classifier supports\n103:         multi-class multi-output.\n104: \n105:         See :term:`multi-output` in the glossary.\n106: \n107:     single_output : bool, default=True\n108:         Whether the target can be single-output. This can be ``False`` if the\n109:         estimator supports only multi-output cases.\n110:     \"\"\"\n111: \n112:     required: bool\n113:     one_d_labels: bool = False\n114:     two_d_labels: bool = False\n115:     positive_only: bool = False\n116:     multi_output: bool = False\n117:     single_output: bool = True\n118: \n119: \n120: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\n121: class TransformerTags:\n122:     \"\"\"Tags for the transformer.\n123: \n124:     Parameters\n125:     ----------\n126:     preserves_dtype : list[str], default=[\"float64\"]\n127:         Applies only on transformers. It corresponds to the data types\n128:         which will be preserved such that `X_trans.dtype` is the same\n129:         as `X.dtype` after calling `transformer.transform(X)`. If this\n130:         list is empty, then the transformer is not expected to\n131:         preserve the data type. The first value in the list is\n132:         considered as the default data type, corresponding to the data\n133:         type of the output when the input data type is not going to be\n134:         preserved.\n135:     \"\"\"\n136: \n137:     preserves_dtype: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: [\"float64\"])\n138: \n139: \n140: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\n141: class ClassifierTags:\n142:     \"\"\"Tags for the classifier.\n143: \n144:     Parameters\n145:     ----------\n146:     poor_score : bool, default=False\n147:         Whether the estimator fails to provide a \"reasonable\" test-set\n148:         score, which currently for classification is an accuracy of\n149:         0.83 on ``make_blobs(n_samples=300, random_state=0)``. The\n150:         datasets and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\n151:         and might be replaced by something more systematic.\n152: \n153:     multi_class : bool, default=True\n154:         Whether the classifier can handle multi-class\n155:         classification. Note that all classifiers support binary\n156:         classification. Therefore this flag indicates whether the\n157:         classifier is a binary-classifier-only or not.\n158: \n159:         See :term:`multi-class` in the glossary.\n160: \n161:     multi_label : bool, default=False\n162:         Whether the classifier supports multi-label output: a data point can\n163:         be predicted to belong to a variable number of classes.\n164: \n165:         See :term:`multi-label` in the glossary.\n166:     \"\"\"\n167: \n168:     poor_score: bool = False\n169:     multi_class: bool = True\n170:     multi_label: bool = False\n171: \n172: \n173: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\n174: class RegressorTags:\n175:     \"\"\"Tags for the regressor.\n176: \n177:     Parameters\n178:     ----------\n179:     poor_score : bool, default=False\n180:         Whether the estimator fails to provide a \"reasonable\" test-set\n181:         score, which currently for regression is an R2 of 0.5 on\n182:         ``make_regression(n_samples=200, n_features=10,\n183:         n_informative=1, bias=5.0, noise=20, random_state=42)``. The\n184:         dataset and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\n185:         and might be replaced by something more systematic.\n186:     \"\"\"\n187: \n188:     poor_score: bool = False\n189: \n190: \n191: @dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\n192: class Tags:\n193:     \"\"\"Tags for the estimator.\n194: \n195:     See :ref:`estimator_tags` for more information.\n196: \n197:     Parameters\n198:     ----------\n199:     estimator_type : str or None\n200:         The type of the estimator. Can be one of:\n201:         - \"classifier\"\n202:         - \"regressor\"\n203:         - \"transformer\"\n204:         - \"clusterer\"\n205:         - \"outlier_detector\"\n206:         - \"density_estimator\"\n207: \n208:     target_tags : :class:`TargetTags`\n209:         The target(y) tags.\n210: \n211:     transformer_tags : :class:`TransformerTags` or None\n212:         The transformer tags.\n213: \n214:     classifier_tags : :class:`ClassifierTags` or None\n215:         The classifier tags.\n216: \n217:     regressor_tags : :class:`RegressorTags` or None\n218:         The regressor tags.\n219: \n220:     array_api_support : bool, default=False\n221:         Whether the estimator supports Array API compatible inputs.\n222: \n223:     no_validation : bool, default=False\n224:         Whether the estimator skips input-validation. This is only meant for\n225:         stateless and dummy transformers!\n226: \n227:     non_deterministic : bool, default=False\n228:         Whether the estimator is not deterministic given a fixed ``random_state``.\n229: \n230:     requires_fit : bool, default=True\n231:         Whether the estimator requires to be fitted before calling one of\n232:         `transform`, `predict`, `predict_proba`, or `decision_function`.\n233: \n234:     _skip_test : bool, default=False\n235:         Whether to skip common tests entirely. Don't use this unless\n236:         you have a *very good* reason.\n237: \n238:     input_tags : :class:`InputTags`\n239:         The input data(X) tags.\n240:     \"\"\"\n241: \n242:     estimator_type: str | None\n243:     target_tags: TargetTags\n244:     transformer_tags: TransformerTags | None = None\n245:     classifier_tags: ClassifierTags | None = None\n246:     regressor_tags: RegressorTags | None = None\n247:     array_api_support: bool = False\n248:     no_validation: bool = False\n249:     non_deterministic: bool = False\n250:     requires_fit: bool = True\n251:     _skip_test: bool = False\n252:     input_tags: InputTags = field(default_factory=InputTags)\n253: \n254: \n255: # TODO(1.8): Remove this function\n256: def default_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\n257:     \"\"\"Get the default tags for an estimator.\n258: \n259:     This ignores any ``__sklearn_tags__`` method that the estimator may have.\n260: \n261:     If the estimator is a classifier or a regressor, ``target_tags.required``\n262:     will be set to ``True``, otherwise it will be set to ``False``.\n263: \n264:     ``transformer_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils. TransformerTags` if the\n265:     estimator has a ``transform`` or ``fit_transform`` method, otherwise it will be set\n266:     to ``None``.\n267: \n268:     ``classifier_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.ClassifierTags` if the\n269:     estimator is a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n270:     a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n271: \n272:     ``regressor_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.RegressorTags` if the\n273:     estimator is a regressor, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n274: \n275:     Parameters\n276:     ----------\n277:     estimator : estimator object\n278:         The estimator for which to get the default tags.\n279: \n280:     Returns\n281:     -------\n282:     tags : Tags\n283:         The default tags for the estimator.\n284:     \"\"\"\n285:     est_is_classifier = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None) == \"classifier\"\n286:     est_is_regressor = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None) == \"regressor\"\n287:     target_required = est_is_classifier or est_is_regressor\n288: \n289:     return Tags(\n290:         estimator_type=getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None),\n291:         target_tags=TargetTags(required=target_required),\n292:         transformer_tags=(\n293:             TransformerTags()\n294:             if hasattr(estimator, \"transform\") or hasattr(estimator, \"fit_transform\")\n295:             else None\n296:         ),\n297:         classifier_tags=ClassifierTags() if est_is_classifier else None,\n298:         regressor_tags=RegressorTags() if est_is_regressor else None,\n299:     )\n300: \n301: \n302: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\n303: def _find_tags_provider(estimator, warn=True):\n304:     \"\"\"Find the tags provider for an estimator.\n305: \n306:     Parameters\n307:     ----------\n308:     estimator : estimator object\n309:         The estimator to find the tags provider for.\n310: \n311:     warn : bool, default=True\n312:         Whether to warn if the tags provider is not found.\n313: \n314:     Returns\n315:     -------\n316:     tag_provider : str\n317:         The tags provider for the estimator. Can be one of:\n318:         - \"_get_tags\": to use the old tags infrastructure\n319:         - \"__sklearn_tags__\": to use the new tags infrastructure\n320:     \"\"\"\n321:     mro_model = type(estimator).mro()\n322:     tags_mro = OrderedDict()\n323:     for klass in mro_model:\n324:         tags_provider = []\n325:         if \"_more_tags\" in vars(klass):\n326:             tags_provider.append(\"_more_tags\")\n327:         if \"_get_tags\" in vars(klass):\n328:             tags_provider.append(\"_get_tags\")\n329:         if \"__sklearn_tags__\" in vars(klass):\n330:             tags_provider.append(\"__sklearn_tags__\")\n331:         tags_mro[klass.__name__] = tags_provider\n332: \n333:     all_providers = set(chain.from_iterable(tags_mro.values()))\n334:     if \"__sklearn_tags__\" not in all_providers:\n335:         # default on the old tags infrastructure\n336:         return \"_get_tags\"\n337: \n338:     tag_provider = \"__sklearn_tags__\"\n339:     for klass in tags_mro:\n340:         has_get_or_more_tags = any(\n341:             provider in tags_mro[klass] for provider in (\"_get_tags\", \"_more_tags\")\n342:         )\n343:         has_sklearn_tags = \"__sklearn_tags__\" in tags_mro[klass]\n344: \n345:         if tags_mro[klass] and tag_provider == \"__sklearn_tags__\":  # is it empty\n346:             if has_get_or_more_tags and not has_sklearn_tags:\n347:                 # Case where a class does not implement __sklearn_tags__ and we fallback\n348:                 # to _get_tags. We should therefore warn for implementing\n349:                 # __sklearn_tags__.\n350:                 tag_provider = \"_get_tags\"\n351:                 break\n352: \n353:     if warn and tag_provider == \"_get_tags\":\n354:         warnings.warn(\n355:             f\"The {estimator.__class__.__name__} or classes from which it inherits \"\n356:             \"use `_get_tags` and `_more_tags`. Please define the \"\n357:             \"`__sklearn_tags__` method, or inherit from `sklearn.base.BaseEstimator` \"\n358:             \"and/or other appropriate mixins such as `sklearn.base.TransformerMixin`, \"\n359:             \"`sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`, `sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`, and \"\n360:             \"`sklearn.base.OutlierMixin`. From scikit-learn 1.7, not defining \"\n361:             \"`__sklearn_tags__` will raise an error.\",\n362:             category=DeprecationWarning,\n363:         )\n364:     return tag_provider\n365: \n366: \n367: def get_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\n368:     \"\"\"Get estimator tags.\n369: \n370:     :class:`~sklearn.BaseEstimator` provides the estimator tags machinery.\n371:     However, if an estimator does not inherit from this base class, we should\n372:     fall-back to the default tags.\n373: \n374:     For scikit-learn built-in estimators, we should still rely on\n375:     `self.__sklearn_tags__()`. `get_tags(est)` should be used when we\n376:     are not sure where `est` comes from: typically\n377:     `get_tags(self.estimator)` where `self` is a meta-estimator, or in\n378:     the common checks.\n379: \n380:     .. versionadded:: 1.6\n381: \n382:     Parameters\n383:     ----------\n384:     estimator : estimator object\n385:         The estimator from which to get the tag.\n386: \n387:     Returns\n388:     -------\n389:     tags : :class:`~.sklearn.utils.Tags`\n390:         The estimator tags.\n391:     \"\"\"\n392: \n393:     tag_provider = _find_tags_provider(estimator)\n394: \n395:     if tag_provider == \"__sklearn_tags__\":\n396:         # TODO(1.7): turn the warning into an error\n397:         try:\n398:             tags = estimator.__sklearn_tags__()\n399:         except AttributeError as exc:\n400:             if str(exc) == \"'super' object has no attribute '__sklearn_tags__'\":\n401:                 # workaround the regression reported in\n402:                 # https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\n403:                 # `__sklearn_tags__` is implemented by calling\n404:                 # `super().__sklearn_tags__()` but there is no `__sklearn_tags__`\n405:                 # method in the base class.\n406:                 warnings.warn(\n407:                     f\"The following error was raised: {str(exc)}. It seems that \"\n408:                     \"there are no classes that implement `__sklearn_tags__` \"\n409:                     \"in the MRO and/or all classes in the MRO call \"\n410:                     \"`super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Make sure to inherit from \"\n411:                     \"`BaseEstimator` which implements `__sklearn_tags__` (or \"\n412:                     \"alternatively define `__sklearn_tags__` but we don't recommend \"\n413:                     \"this approach). Note that `BaseEstimator` needs to be on the \"\n414:                     \"right side of other Mixins in the inheritance order. The \"\n415:                     \"default are now used instead since retrieving tags failed. \"\n416:                     \"This warning will be replaced by an error in 1.7.\",\n417:                     category=DeprecationWarning,\n418:                 )\n419:                 tags = default_tags(estimator)\n420:             else:\n421:                 raise\n422:     else:\n423:         # TODO(1.7): Remove this branch of the code\n424:         # Let's go through the MRO and patch each class implementing _more_tags\n425:         sklearn_tags_provider = {}\n426:         more_tags_provider = {}\n427:         class_order = []\n428:         for klass in reversed(type(estimator).mro()):\n429:             if \"__sklearn_tags__\" in vars(klass):\n430:                 sklearn_tags_provider[klass] = klass.__sklearn_tags__(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n431:                 class_order.append(klass)\n432:             elif \"_more_tags\" in vars(klass):\n433:                 more_tags_provider[klass] = klass._more_tags(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n434:                 class_order.append(klass)\n435: \n436:         # Find differences between consecutive in the case of __sklearn_tags__\n437:         # inheritance\n438:         sklearn_tags_diff = {}\n439:         items = list(sklearn_tags_provider.items())\n440:         for current_item, next_item in zip(items[:-1], items[1:]):\n441:             current_name, current_tags = current_item\n442:             next_name, next_tags = next_item\n443:             current_tags = _to_old_tags(current_tags)\n444:             next_tags = _to_old_tags(next_tags)\n445: \n446:             # Compare tags and store differences\n447:             diff = {}\n448:             for key in current_tags:\n449:                 if current_tags[key] != next_tags[key]:\n450:                     diff[key] = next_tags[key]\n451: \n452:             sklearn_tags_diff[next_name] = diff\n453: \n454:         tags = {}\n455:         for klass in class_order:\n456:             if klass in sklearn_tags_diff:\n457:                 tags.update(sklearn_tags_diff[klass])\n458:             elif klass in more_tags_provider:\n459:                 tags.update(more_tags_provider[klass])\n460: \n461:         tags = _to_new_tags(\n462:             {**_to_old_tags(default_tags(estimator)), **tags}, estimator\n463:         )\n464: \n465:     return tags\n466: \n467: \n468: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\n469: def _safe_tags(estimator, key=None):\n470:     warnings.warn(\n471:         \"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed in \"\n472:         \"1.7. Use the public `get_tags` function instead and make sure to implement \"\n473:         \"the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\",\n474:         category=DeprecationWarning,\n475:     )\n476:     tags = _to_old_tags(get_tags(estimator))\n477: \n478:     if key is not None:\n479:         if key not in tags:\n480:             raise ValueError(\n481:                 f\"The key {key} is not defined for the class \"\n482:                 f\"{estimator.__class__.__name__}.\"\n483:             )\n484:         return tags[key]\n485:     return tags\n486: \n487: \n488: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\n489: def _to_new_tags(old_tags, estimator=None):\n490:     \"\"\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\"\"\"\n491:     input_tags = InputTags(\n492:         one_d_array=\"1darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n493:         two_d_array=\"2darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n494:         three_d_array=\"3darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n495:         sparse=\"sparse\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n496:         categorical=\"categorical\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n497:         string=\"string\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n498:         dict=\"dict\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n499:         positive_only=old_tags[\"requires_positive_X\"],\n500:         allow_nan=old_tags[\"allow_nan\"],\n501:         pairwise=old_tags[\"pairwise\"],\n502:     )\n503:     target_tags = TargetTags(\n504:         required=old_tags[\"requires_y\"],\n505:         one_d_labels=\"1dlabels\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n506:         two_d_labels=\"2dlabels\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n507:         positive_only=old_tags[\"requires_positive_y\"],\n508:         multi_output=old_tags[\"multioutput\"] or old_tags[\"multioutput_only\"],\n509:         single_output=not old_tags[\"multioutput_only\"],\n510:     )\n511:     if estimator is not None and (\n512:         hasattr(estimator, \"transform\") or hasattr(estimator, \"fit_transform\")\n513:     ):\n514:         transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\n515:             preserves_dtype=old_tags[\"preserves_dtype\"],\n516:         )\n517:     else:\n518:         transformer_tags = None\n519:     estimator_type = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None)\n520:     if estimator_type == \"classifier\":\n521:         classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\n522:             poor_score=old_tags[\"poor_score\"],\n523:             multi_class=not old_tags[\"binary_only\"],\n524:             multi_label=old_tags[\"multilabel\"],\n525:         )\n526:     else:\n527:         classifier_tags = None\n528:     if estimator_type == \"regressor\":\n529:         regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\n530:             poor_score=old_tags[\"poor_score\"],\n531:         )\n532:     else:\n533:         regressor_tags = None\n534:     return Tags(\n535:         estimator_type=estimator_type,\n536:         target_tags=target_tags,\n537:         transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n538:         classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n539:         regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n540:         input_tags=input_tags,\n541:         array_api_support=old_tags[\"array_api_support\"],\n542:         no_validation=old_tags[\"no_validation\"],\n543:         non_deterministic=old_tags[\"non_deterministic\"],\n544:         requires_fit=old_tags[\"requires_fit\"],\n545:         _skip_test=old_tags[\"_skip_test\"],\n546:     )\n547: \n548: \n549: # TODO(1.7): Remove this function\n550: def _to_old_tags(new_tags):\n551:     \"\"\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\"\"\"\n552:     if new_tags.classifier_tags:\n553:         binary_only = not new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_class\n554:         multilabel = new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_label\n555:         poor_score_clf = new_tags.classifier_tags.poor_score\n556:     else:\n557:         binary_only = False\n558:         multilabel = False\n559:         poor_score_clf = False\n560: \n561:     if new_tags.regressor_tags:\n562:         poor_score_reg = new_tags.regressor_tags.poor_score\n563:     else:\n564:         poor_score_reg = False\n565: \n566:     if new_tags.transformer_tags:\n567:         preserves_dtype = new_tags.transformer_tags.preserves_dtype\n568:     else:\n569:         preserves_dtype = [\"float64\"]\n570: \n571:     tags = {\n572:         \"allow_nan\": new_tags.input_tags.allow_nan,\n573:         \"array_api_support\": new_tags.array_api_support,\n574:         \"binary_only\": binary_only,\n575:         \"multilabel\": multilabel,\n576:         \"multioutput\": new_tags.target_tags.multi_output,\n577:         \"multioutput_only\": (\n578:             not new_tags.target_tags.single_output and new_tags.target_tags.multi_output\n579:         ),\n580:         \"no_validation\": new_tags.no_validation,\n581:         \"non_deterministic\": new_tags.non_deterministic,\n582:         \"pairwise\": new_tags.input_tags.pairwise,\n583:         \"preserves_dtype\": preserves_dtype,\n584:         \"poor_score\": poor_score_clf or poor_score_reg,\n585:         \"requires_fit\": new_tags.requires_fit,\n586:         \"requires_positive_X\": new_tags.input_tags.positive_only,\n587:         \"requires_y\": new_tags.target_tags.required,\n588:         \"requires_positive_y\": new_tags.target_tags.positive_only,\n589:         \"_skip_test\": new_tags._skip_test,\n590:         \"stateless\": new_tags.requires_fit,\n591:     }\n592:     X_types = []\n593:     if new_tags.input_tags.one_d_array:\n594:         X_types.append(\"1darray\")\n595:     if new_tags.input_tags.two_d_array:\n596:         X_types.append(\"2darray\")\n597:     if new_tags.input_tags.three_d_array:\n598:         X_types.append(\"3darray\")\n599:     if new_tags.input_tags.sparse:\n600:         X_types.append(\"sparse\")\n601:     if new_tags.input_tags.categorical:\n602:         X_types.append(\"categorical\")\n603:     if new_tags.input_tags.string:\n604:         X_types.append(\"string\")\n605:     if new_tags.input_tags.dict:\n606:         X_types.append(\"dict\")\n607:     if new_tags.target_tags.one_d_labels:\n608:         X_types.append(\"1dlabels\")\n609:     if new_tags.target_tags.two_d_labels:\n610:         X_types.append(\"2dlabels\")\n611:     tags[\"X_types\"] = X_types\n612:     return tags\n613: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/sklearn/utils/_tags.py"
  }
}
22. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport warnings\nfrom collections import OrderedDict\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom itertools import chain\n\nfrom .fixes import _dataclass_args\n\n# Authors: The scikit-learn developers\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass InputTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the input data.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    one_d_array : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a 1D array.\n\n    two_d_array : bool, default=True\n        Whether the input can be a 2D array. Note that most common\n        tests currently run only if this flag is set to ``True``.\n\n    three_d_array : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a 3D array.\n\n    sparse : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a sparse matrix.\n\n    categorical : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be categorical.\n\n    string : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be an array-like of strings.\n\n    dict : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a dictionary.\n\n    positive_only : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator requires positive X.\n\n    allow_nan : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator supports data with missing values encoded as `np.nan`.\n\n    pairwise : bool, default=False\n        This boolean attribute indicates whether the data (`X`),\n        :term:`fit` and similar methods consists of pairwise measures\n        over samples rather than a feature representation for each\n        sample.  It is usually `True` where an estimator has a\n        `metric` or `affinity` or `kernel` parameter with value\n        'precomputed'. Its primary purpose is to support a\n        :term:`meta-estimator` or a cross validation procedure that\n        extracts a sub-sample of data intended for a pairwise\n        estimator, where the data needs to be indexed on both axes.\n        Specifically, this tag is used by\n        `sklearn.utils.metaestimators._safe_split` to slice rows and\n        columns.\n\n        Note that if setting this tag to ``True`` means the estimator can take only\n        positive values, the `positive_only` tag must reflect it and also be set to\n        ``True``.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    one_d_array: bool = False\n    two_d_array: bool = True\n    three_d_array: bool = False\n    sparse: bool = False\n    categorical: bool = False\n    string: bool = False\n    dict: bool = False\n    positive_only: bool = False\n    allow_nan: bool = False\n    pairwise: bool = False\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass TargetTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the target data.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    required : bool\n        Whether the estimator requires y to be passed to `fit`,\n        `fit_predict` or `fit_transform` methods. The tag is ``True``\n        for estimators inheriting from `~sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`\n        and `~sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`.\n\n    one_d_labels : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input is a 1D labels (y).\n\n    two_d_labels : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input is a 2D labels (y).\n\n    positive_only : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator requires a positive y (only applicable\n        for regression).\n\n    multi_output : bool, default=False\n        Whether a regressor supports multi-target outputs or a classifier supports\n        multi-class multi-output.\n\n        See :term:`multi-output` in the glossary.\n\n    single_output : bool, default=True\n        Whether the target can be single-output. This can be ``False`` if the\n        estimator supports only multi-output cases.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    required: bool\n    one_d_labels: bool = False\n    two_d_labels: bool = False\n    positive_only: bool = False\n    multi_output: bool = False\n    single_output: bool = True\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass TransformerTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the transformer.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    preserves_dtype : list[str], default=[\"float64\"]\n        Applies only on transformers. It corresponds to the data types\n        which will be preserved such that `X_trans.dtype` is the same\n        as `X.dtype` after calling `transformer.transform(X)`. If this\n        list is empty, then the transformer is not expected to\n        preserve the data type. The first value in the list is\n        considered as the default data type, corresponding to the data\n        type of the output when the input data type is not going to be\n        preserved.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    preserves_dtype: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: [\"float64\"])\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass ClassifierTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the classifier.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    poor_score : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator fails to provide a \"reasonable\" test-set\n        score, which currently for classification is an accuracy of\n        0.83 on ``make_blobs(n_samples=300, random_state=0)``. The\n        datasets and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\n        and might be replaced by something more systematic.\n\n    multi_class : bool, default=True\n        Whether the classifier can handle multi-class\n        classification. Note that all classifiers support binary\n        classification. Therefore this flag indicates whether the\n        classifier is a binary-classifier-only or not.\n\n        See :term:`multi-class` in the glossary.\n\n    multi_label : bool, default=False\n        Whether the classifier supports multi-label output: a data point can\n        be predicted to belong to a variable number of classes.\n\n        See :term:`multi-label` in the glossary.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    poor_score: bool = False\n    multi_class: bool = True\n    multi_label: bool = False\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass RegressorTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the regressor.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    poor_score : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator fails to provide a \"reasonable\" test-set\n        score, which currently for regression is an R2 of 0.5 on\n        ``make_regression(n_samples=200, n_features=10,\n        n_informative=1, bias=5.0, noise=20, random_state=42)``. The\n        dataset and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\n        and might be replaced by something more systematic.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    poor_score: bool = False\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass Tags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the estimator.\n\n    See :ref:`estimator_tags` for more information.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator_type : str or None\n        The type of the estimator. Can be one of:\n        - \"classifier\"\n        - \"regressor\"\n        - \"transformer\"\n        - \"clusterer\"\n        - \"outlier_detector\"\n        - \"density_estimator\"\n\n    target_tags : :class:`TargetTags`\n        The target(y) tags.\n\n    transformer_tags : :class:`TransformerTags` or None\n        The transformer tags.\n\n    classifier_tags : :class:`ClassifierTags` or None\n        The classifier tags.\n\n    regressor_tags : :class:`RegressorTags` or None\n        The regressor tags.\n\n    array_api_support : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator supports Array API compatible inputs.\n\n    no_validation : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator skips input-validation. This is only meant for\n        stateless and dummy transformers!\n\n    non_deterministic : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator is not deterministic given a fixed ``random_state``.\n\n    requires_fit : bool, default=True\n        Whether the estimator requires to be fitted before calling one of\n        `transform`, `predict`, `predict_proba`, or `decision_function`.\n\n    _skip_test : bool, default=False\n        Whether to skip common tests entirely. Don't use this unless\n        you have a *very good* reason.\n\n    input_tags : :class:`InputTags`\n        The input data(X) tags.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    estimator_type: str | None\n    target_tags: TargetTags\n    transformer_tags: TransformerTags | None = None\n    classifier_tags: ClassifierTags | None = None\n    regressor_tags: RegressorTags | None = None\n    array_api_support: bool = False\n    no_validation: bool = False\n    non_deterministic: bool = False\n    requires_fit: bool = True\n    _skip_test: bool = False\n    input_tags: InputTags = field(default_factory=InputTags)\n\n\n# TODO(1.8): Remove this function\ndef default_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\n    \"\"\"Get the default tags for an estimator.\n\n    This ignores any ``__sklearn_tags__`` method that the estimator may have.\n\n    If the estimator is a classifier or a regressor, ``target_tags.required``\n    will be set to ``True``, otherwise it will be set to ``False``.\n\n    ``transformer_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils. TransformerTags` if the\n    estimator has a ``transform`` or ``fit_transform`` method, otherwise it will be set\n    to ``None``.\n\n    ``classifier_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.ClassifierTags` if the\n    estimator is a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n    a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n\n    ``regressor_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.RegressorTags` if the\n    estimator is a regressor, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator : estimator object\n        The estimator for which to get the default tags.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    tags : Tags\n        The default tags for the estimator.\n    \"\"\"\n    est_is_classifier = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None) == \"classifier\"\n    est_is_regressor = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None) == \"regressor\"\n    target_required = est_is_classifier or est_is_regressor\n\n    return Tags(\n        estimator_type=getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None),\n        target_tags=TargetTags(required=target_required),\n        transformer_tags=(\n            TransformerTags()\n            if hasattr(estimator, \"transform\") or hasattr(estimator, \"fit_transform\")\n            else None\n        ),\n        classifier_tags=ClassifierTags() if est_is_classifier else None,\n        regressor_tags=RegressorTags() if est_is_regressor else None,\n    )\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _find_tags_provider(estimator, warn=True):\n    \"\"\"Find the tags provider for an estimator.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator : estimator object\n        The estimator to find the tags provider for.\n\n    warn : bool, default=True\n        Whether to warn if the tags provider is not found.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    tag_provider : str\n        The tags provider for the estimator. Can be one of:\n        - \"_estimator_tags\": to use the old tags infrastructure\n        - \"__sklearn_tags__\": to use the new tags infrastructure\n    \"\"\"\n    mro_model = type(estimator).mro()\n    tags_mro = OrderedDict()\n    for klass in mro_model:\n        tags_provider = []\n        if \"_more_tags\" in vars(klass):\n            tags_provider.append(\"_more_tags\")\n        if \"_estimator_tags\" in vars(klass):\n            tags_provider.append(\"_estimator_tags\")\n        if \"__sklearn_tags__\" in vars(klass):\n            tags_provider.append(\"__sklearn_tags__\")\n        tags_mro[klass.__name__] = tags_provider\n\n    all_providers = set(chain.from_iterable(tags_mro.values()))\n    if \"__sklearn_tags__\" not in all_providers:\n        # default on the old tags infrastructure\n        return \"_estimator_tags\"\n\n    tag_provider = \"__sklearn_tags__\"\n    for klass in tags_mro:\n        has_get_or_more_tags = any(\n            provider in tags_mro[klass] for provider in (\"_estimator_tags\", \"_more_tags\")\n        )\n        has_sklearn_tags = \"__sklearn_tags__\" in tags_mro[klass]\n\n        if tags_mro[klass] and tag_provider == \"__sklearn_tags__\":  # is it empty\n            if has_get_or_more_tags and not has_sklearn_tags:\n                # Case where a class does not implement __sklearn_tags__ and we fallback\n                # to _estimator_tags. We should therefore warn for implementing\n                # __sklearn_tags__.\n                tag_provider = \"_estimator_tags\"\n                break\n\n    if warn and tag_provider == \"_estimator_tags\":\n        warnings.warn(\n            f\"The {estimator.__class__.__name__} or classes from which it inherits \"\n            \"use `_estimator_tags` and `_more_tags`. Please define the \"\n            \"`__sklearn_tags__` method, or inherit from `sklearn.base.BaseEstimator` \"\n            \"and/or other appropriate mixins such as `sklearn.base.TransformerMixin`, \"\n            \"`sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`, `sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`, and \"\n            \"`sklearn.base.OutlierMixin`. From scikit-learn 1.7, not defining \"\n            \"`__sklearn_tags__` will raise an error.\",\n            category=DeprecationWarning,\n        )\n    return tag_provider\n\n\ndef get_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\n    \"\"\"Get estimator tags.\n\n    :class:`~sklearn.BaseEstimator` provides the estimator tags machinery.\n    However, if an estimator does not inherit from this base class, we should\n    fall-back to the default tags.\n\n    For scikit-learn built-in estimators, we should still rely on\n    `self.__sklearn_tags__()`. `get_tags(est)` should be used when we\n    are not sure where `est` comes from: typically\n    `get_tags(self.estimator)` where `self` is a meta-estimator, or in\n    the common checks.\n\n    .. versionadded:: 1.6\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator : estimator object\n        The estimator from which to get the tag.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    tags : :class:`~.sklearn.utils.Tags`\n        The estimator tags.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    tag_provider = _find_tags_provider(estimator)\n\n    if tag_provider == \"__sklearn_tags__\":\n        # TODO(1.7): turn the warning into an error\n        try:\n            tags = estimator.__sklearn_tags__()\n        except AttributeError as exc:\n            if str(exc) == \"'super' object has no attribute '__sklearn_tags__'\":\n                # workaround the regression reported in\n                # https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\n                # `__sklearn_tags__` is implemented by calling\n                # `super().__sklearn_tags__()` but there is no `__sklearn_tags__`\n                # method in the base class.\n                warnings.warn(\n                    f\"The following error was raised: {str(exc)}. It seems that \"\n                    \"there are no classes that implement `__sklearn_tags__` \"\n                    \"in the MRO and/or all classes in the MRO call \"\n                    \"`super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Make sure to inherit from \"\n                    \"`BaseEstimator` which implements `__sklearn_tags__` (or \"\n                    \"alternatively define `__sklearn_tags__` but we don't recommend \"\n                    \"this approach). Note that `BaseEstimator` needs to be on the \"\n                    \"right side of other Mixins in the inheritance order. The \"\n                    \"default are now used instead since retrieving tags failed. \"\n                    \"This warning will be replaced by an error in 1.7.\",\n                    category=DeprecationWarning,\n                )\n                tags = default_tags(estimator)\n            else:\n                raise\n    else:\n        # TODO(1.7): Remove this branch of the code\n        # Let's go through the MRO and patch each class implementing _more_tags\n        sklearn_tags_provider = {}\n        more_tags_provider = {}\n        class_order = []\n        for klass in reversed(type(estimator).mro()):\n            if \"__sklearn_tags__\" in vars(klass):\n                sklearn_tags_provider[klass] = klass.__sklearn_tags__(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                class_order.append(klass)\n            elif \"_more_tags\" in vars(klass):\n                more_tags_provider[klass] = klass._more_tags(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                class_order.append(klass)\n\n        # Find differences between consecutive in the case of __sklearn_tags__\n        # inheritance\n        sklearn_tags_diff = {}\n        items = list(sklearn_tags_provider.items())\n        for current_item, next_item in zip(items[:-1], items[1:]):\n            current_name, current_tags = current_item\n            next_name, next_tags = next_item\n            current_tags = _to_old_tags(current_tags)\n            next_tags = _to_old_tags(next_tags)\n\n            # Compare tags and store differences\n            diff = {}\n            for key in current_tags:\n                if current_tags[key] != next_tags[key]:\n                    diff[key] = next_tags[key]\n\n            sklearn_tags_diff[next_name] = diff\n\n        tags = {}\n        for klass in class_order:\n            if klass in sklearn_tags_diff:\n                tags.update(sklearn_tags_diff[klass])\n            elif klass in more_tags_provider:\n                tags.update(more_tags_provider[klass])\n\n        tags = _to_new_tags(\n            {**_to_old_tags(default_tags(estimator)), **tags}, estimator\n        )\n\n    return tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _safe_tags(estimator, key=None):\n    warnings.warn(\n        \"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed in \"\n        \"1.7. Use the public `get_tags` function instead and make sure to implement \"\n        \"the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\",\n        category=DeprecationWarning,\n    )\n    tags = _to_old_tags(get_tags(estimator))\n\n    if key is not None:\n        if key not in tags:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"The key {key} is not defined for the class \"\n                f\"{estimator.__class__.__name__}.\"\n            )\n        return tags[key]\n    return tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _to_new_tags(old_tags, estimator=None):\n    \"\"\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\"\"\"\n    input_tags = InputTags(\n        one_d_array=\"1darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        two_d_array=\"2darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        three_d_array=\"3darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        sparse=\"sparse\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        categorical=\"categorical\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        string=\"string\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        dict=\"dict\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        positive_only=old_tags[\"requires_positive_X\"],\n        allow_nan=old_tags[\"allow_nan\"],\n        pairwise=old_tags[\"pairwise\"],\n    )\n    target_tags = TargetTags(\n        required=old_tags[\"requires_y\"],\n        one_d_labels=\"1dlabels\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        two_d_labels=\"2dlabels\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        positive_only=old_tags[\"requires_positive_y\"],\n        multi_output=old_tags[\"multioutput\"] or old_tags[\"multioutput_only\"],\n        single_output=not old_tags[\"multioutput_only\"],\n    )\n    if estimator is not None and (\n        hasattr(estimator, \"transform\") or hasattr(estimator, \"fit_transform\")\n    ):\n        transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\n            preserves_dtype=old_tags[\"preserves_dtype\"],\n        )\n    else:\n        transformer_tags = None\n    estimator_type = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None)\n    if estimator_type == \"classifier\":\n        classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\n            poor_score=old_tags[\"poor_score\"],\n            multi_class=not old_tags[\"binary_only\"],\n            multi_label=old_tags[\"multilabel\"],\n        )\n    else:\n        classifier_tags = None\n    if estimator_type == \"regressor\":\n        regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\n            poor_score=old_tags[\"poor_score\"],\n        )\n    else:\n        regressor_tags = None\n    return Tags(\n        estimator_type=estimator_type,\n        target_tags=target_tags,\n        transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n        classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n        regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n        input_tags=input_tags,\n        array_api_support=old_tags[\"array_api_support\"],\n        no_validation=old_tags[\"no_validation\"],\n        non_deterministic=old_tags[\"non_deterministic\"],\n        requires_fit=old_tags[\"requires_fit\"],\n        _skip_test=old_tags[\"_skip_test\"],\n    )\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _to_old_tags(new_tags):\n    \"\"\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\"\"\"\n    if new_tags.classifier_tags:\n        binary_only = not new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_class\n        multilabel = new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_label\n        poor_score_clf = new_tags.classifier_tags.poor_score\n    else:\n        binary_only = False\n        multilabel = False\n        poor_score_clf = False\n\n    if new_tags.regressor_tags:\n        poor_score_reg = new_tags.regressor_tags.poor_score\n    else:\n        poor_score_reg = False\n\n    if new_tags.transformer_tags:\n        preserves_dtype = new_tags.transformer_tags.preserves_dtype\n    else:\n        preserves_dtype = [\"float64\"]\n\n    tags = {\n        \"allow_nan\": new_tags.input_tags.allow_nan,\n        \"array_api_support\": new_tags.array_api_support,\n        \"binary_only\": binary_only,\n        \"multilabel\": multilabel,\n        \"multioutput\": new_tags.target_tags.multi_output,\n        \"multioutput_only\": (\n            not new_tags.target_tags.single_output and new_tags.target_tags.multi_output\n        ),\n        \"no_validation\": new_tags.no_validation,\n        \"non_deterministic\": new_tags.non_deterministic,\n        \"pairwise\": new_tags.input_tags.pairwise,\n        \"preserves_dtype\": preserves_dtype,\n        \"poor_score\": poor_score_clf or poor_score_reg,\n        \"requires_fit\": new_tags.requires_fit,\n        \"requires_positive_X\": new_tags.input_tags.positive_only,\n        \"requires_y\": new_tags.target_tags.required,\n        \"requires_positive_y\": new_tags.target_tags.positive_only,\n        \"_skip_test\": new_tags._skip_test,\n        \"stateless\": new_tags.requires_fit,\n    }\n    X_types = []\n    if new_tags.input_tags.one_d_array:\n        X_types.append(\"1darray\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.two_d_array:\n        X_types.append(\"2darray\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.three_d_array:\n        X_types.append(\"3darray\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.sparse:\n        X_types.append(\"sparse\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.categorical:\n        X_types.append(\"categorical\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.string:\n        X_types.append(\"string\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.dict:\n        X_types.append(\"dict\")\n    if new_tags.target_tags.one_d_labels:\n        X_types.append(\"1dlabels\")\n    if new_tags.target_tags.two_d_labels:\n        X_types.append(\"2dlabels\")\n    tags[\"X_types\"] = X_types\n    return tags\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/sklearn/utils/_tags.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport warnings\nfrom collections import OrderedDict\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom itertools import chain\n\nfrom .fixes import _dataclass_args\n\n# Authors: The scikit-learn developers\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass InputTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the input data.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    one_d_array : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a 1D array.\n\n    two_d_array : bool, default=True\n        Whether the input can be a 2D array. Note that most common\n        tests currently run only if this flag is set to ``True``.\n\n    three_d_array : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a 3D array.\n\n    sparse : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a sparse matrix.\n\n    categorical : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be categorical.\n\n    string : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be an array-like of strings.\n\n    dict : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input can be a dictionary.\n\n    positive_only : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator requires positive X.\n\n    allow_nan : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator supports data with missing values encoded as `np.nan`.\n\n    pairwise : bool, default=False\n        This boolean attribute indicates whether the data (`X`),\n        :term:`fit` and similar methods consists of pairwise measures\n        over samples rather than a feature representation for each\n        sample.  It is usually `True` where an estimator has a\n        `metric` or `affinity` or `kernel` parameter with value\n        'precomputed'. Its primary purpose is to support a\n        :term:`meta-estimator` or a cross validation procedure that\n        extracts a sub-sample of data intended for a pairwise\n        estimator, where the data needs to be indexed on both axes.\n        Specifically, this tag is used by\n        `sklearn.utils.metaestimators._safe_split` to slice rows and\n        columns.\n\n        Note that if setting this tag to ``True`` means the estimator can take only\n        positive values, the `positive_only` tag must reflect it and also be set to\n        ``True``.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    one_d_array: bool = False\n    two_d_array: bool = True\n    three_d_array: bool = False\n    sparse: bool = False\n    categorical: bool = False\n    string: bool = False\n    dict: bool = False\n    positive_only: bool = False\n    allow_nan: bool = False\n    pairwise: bool = False\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass TargetTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the target data.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    required : bool\n        Whether the estimator requires y to be passed to `fit`,\n        `fit_predict` or `fit_transform` methods. The tag is ``True``\n        for estimators inheriting from `~sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`\n        and `~sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`.\n\n    one_d_labels : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input is a 1D labels (y).\n\n    two_d_labels : bool, default=False\n        Whether the input is a 2D labels (y).\n\n    positive_only : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator requires a positive y (only applicable\n        for regression).\n\n    multi_output : bool, default=False\n        Whether a regressor supports multi-target outputs or a classifier supports\n        multi-class multi-output.\n\n        See :term:`multi-output` in the glossary.\n\n    single_output : bool, default=True\n        Whether the target can be single-output. This can be ``False`` if the\n        estimator supports only multi-output cases.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    required: bool\n    one_d_labels: bool = False\n    two_d_labels: bool = False\n    positive_only: bool = False\n    multi_output: bool = False\n    single_output: bool = True\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass TransformerTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the transformer.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    preserves_dtype : list[str], default=[\"float64\"]\n        Applies only on transformers. It corresponds to the data types\n        which will be preserved such that `X_trans.dtype` is the same\n        as `X.dtype` after calling `transformer.transform(X)`. If this\n        list is empty, then the transformer is not expected to\n        preserve the data type. The first value in the list is\n        considered as the default data type, corresponding to the data\n        type of the output when the input data type is not going to be\n        preserved.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    preserves_dtype: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: [\"float64\"])\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass ClassifierTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the classifier.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    poor_score : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator fails to provide a \"reasonable\" test-set\n        score, which currently for classification is an accuracy of\n        0.83 on ``make_blobs(n_samples=300, random_state=0)``. The\n        datasets and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\n        and might be replaced by something more systematic.\n\n    multi_class : bool, default=True\n        Whether the classifier can handle multi-class\n        classification. Note that all classifiers support binary\n        classification. Therefore this flag indicates whether the\n        classifier is a binary-classifier-only or not.\n\n        See :term:`multi-class` in the glossary.\n\n    multi_label : bool, default=False\n        Whether the classifier supports multi-label output: a data point can\n        be predicted to belong to a variable number of classes.\n\n        See :term:`multi-label` in the glossary.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    poor_score: bool = False\n    multi_class: bool = True\n    multi_label: bool = False\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass RegressorTags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the regressor.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    poor_score : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator fails to provide a \"reasonable\" test-set\n        score, which currently for regression is an R2 of 0.5 on\n        ``make_regression(n_samples=200, n_features=10,\n        n_informative=1, bias=5.0, noise=20, random_state=42)``. The\n        dataset and values are based on current estimators in scikit-learn\n        and might be replaced by something more systematic.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    poor_score: bool = False\n\n\n@dataclass(**_dataclass_args())\nclass Tags:\n    \"\"\"Tags for the estimator.\n\n    See :ref:`estimator_tags` for more information.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator_type : str or None\n        The type of the estimator.
