Can be one of:\n        - \"classifier\"\n        - \"regressor\"\n        - \"transformer\"\n        - \"clusterer\"\n        - \"outlier_detector\"\n        - \"density_estimator\"\n\n    target_tags : :class:`TargetTags`\n        The target(y) tags.\n\n    transformer_tags : :class:`TransformerTags` or None\n        The transformer tags.\n\n    classifier_tags : :class:`ClassifierTags` or None\n        The classifier tags.\n\n    regressor_tags : :class:`RegressorTags` or None\n        The regressor tags.\n\n    array_api_support : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator supports Array API compatible inputs.\n\n    no_validation : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator skips input-validation. This is only meant for\n        stateless and dummy transformers!\n\n    non_deterministic : bool, default=False\n        Whether the estimator is not deterministic given a fixed ``random_state``.\n\n    requires_fit : bool, default=True\n        Whether the estimator requires to be fitted before calling one of\n        `transform`, `predict`, `predict_proba`, or `decision_function`.\n\n    _skip_test : bool, default=False\n        Whether to skip common tests entirely. Don't use this unless\n        you have a *very good* reason.\n\n    input_tags : :class:`InputTags`\n        The input data(X) tags.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    estimator_type: str | None\n    target_tags: TargetTags\n    transformer_tags: TransformerTags | None = None\n    classifier_tags: ClassifierTags | None = None\n    regressor_tags: RegressorTags | None = None\n    array_api_support: bool = False\n    no_validation: bool = False\n    non_deterministic: bool = False\n    requires_fit: bool = True\n    _skip_test: bool = False\n    input_tags: InputTags = field(default_factory=InputTags)\n\n\n# TODO(1.8): Remove this function\ndef default_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\n    \"\"\"Get the default tags for an estimator.\n\n    This ignores any ``__sklearn_tags__`` method that the estimator may have.\n\n    If the estimator is a classifier or a regressor, ``target_tags.required``\n    will be set to ``True``, otherwise it will be set to ``False``.\n\n    ``transformer_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils. TransformerTags` if the\n    estimator has a ``transform`` or ``fit_transform`` method, otherwise it will be set\n    to ``None``.\n\n    ``classifier_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.ClassifierTags` if the\n    estimator is a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n    a classifier, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n\n    ``regressor_tags`` will be set to :class:`~.sklearn.utils.RegressorTags` if the\n    estimator is a regressor, otherwise it will be set to ``None``.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator : estimator object\n        The estimator for which to get the default tags.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    tags : Tags\n        The default tags for the estimator.\n    \"\"\"\n    est_is_classifier = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None) == \"classifier\"\n    est_is_regressor = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None) == \"regressor\"\n    target_required = est_is_classifier or est_is_regressor\n\n    return Tags(\n        estimator_type=getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None),\n        target_tags=TargetTags(required=target_required),\n        transformer_tags=(\n            TransformerTags()\n            if hasattr(estimator, \"transform\") or hasattr(estimator, \"fit_transform\")\n            else None\n        ),\n        classifier_tags=ClassifierTags() if est_is_classifier else None,\n        regressor_tags=RegressorTags() if est_is_regressor else None,\n    )\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _find_tags_provider(estimator, warn=True):\n    \"\"\"Find the tags provider for an estimator.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator : estimator object\n        The estimator to find the tags provider for.\n\n    warn : bool, default=True\n        Whether to warn if the tags provider is not found.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    tag_provider : str\n        The tags provider for the estimator. Can be one of:\n        - \"_estimator_tags\": to use the old tags infrastructure\n        - \"__sklearn_tags__\": to use the new tags infrastructure\n    \"\"\"\n    mro_model = type(estimator).mro()\n    tags_mro = OrderedDict()\n    for klass in mro_model:\n        tags_provider = []\n        if \"_more_tags\" in vars(klass):\n            tags_provider.append(\"_more_tags\")\n        if \"_estimator_tags\" in vars(klass):\n            tags_provider.append(\"_estimator_tags\")\n        if \"__sklearn_tags__\" in vars(klass):\n            tags_provider.append(\"__sklearn_tags__\")\n        tags_mro[klass.__name__] = tags_provider\n\n    all_providers = set(chain.from_iterable(tags_mro.values()))\n    if \"__sklearn_tags__\" not in all_providers:\n        # default on the old tags infrastructure\n        return \"_estimator_tags\"\n\n    tag_provider = \"__sklearn_tags__\"\n    for klass in tags_mro:\n        has_get_or_more_tags = any(\n            provider in tags_mro[klass] for provider in (\"_estimator_tags\", \"_more_tags\")\n        )\n        has_sklearn_tags = \"__sklearn_tags__\" in tags_mro[klass]\n\n        if tags_mro[klass] and tag_provider == \"__sklearn_tags__\":  # is it empty\n            if has_get_or_more_tags and not has_sklearn_tags:\n                # Case where a class does not implement __sklearn_tags__ and we fallback\n                # to _estimator_tags. We should therefore warn for implementing\n                # __sklearn_tags__.\n                tag_provider = \"_estimator_tags\"\n                break\n\n    if warn and tag_provider == \"_estimator_tags\":\n        warnings.warn(\n            f\"The {estimator.__class__.__name__} or classes from which it inherits \"\n            \"use `_estimator_tags` and `_more_tags`. Please define the \"\n            \"`__sklearn_tags__` method, or inherit from `sklearn.base.BaseEstimator` \"\n            \"and/or other appropriate mixins such as `sklearn.base.TransformerMixin`, \"\n            \"`sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`, `sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`, and \"\n            \"`sklearn.base.OutlierMixin`. From scikit-learn 1.7, not defining \"\n            \"`__sklearn_tags__` will raise an error.\",\n            category=DeprecationWarning,\n        )\n    return tag_provider\n\n\ndef get_tags(estimator) -> Tags:\n    \"\"\"Get estimator tags.\n\n    :class:`~sklearn.BaseEstimator` provides the estimator tags machinery.\n    However, if an estimator does not inherit from this base class, we should\n    fall-back to the default tags.\n\n    For scikit-learn built-in estimators, we should still rely on\n    `self.__sklearn_tags__()`. `get_tags(est)` should be used when we\n    are not sure where `est` comes from: typically\n    `get_tags(self.estimator)` where `self` is a meta-estimator, or in\n    the common checks.\n\n    .. versionadded:: 1.6\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    estimator : estimator object\n        The estimator from which to get the tag.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    tags : :class:`~.sklearn.utils.Tags`\n        The estimator tags.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    tag_provider = _find_tags_provider(estimator)\n\n    if tag_provider == \"__sklearn_tags__\":\n        # TODO(1.7): turn the warning into an error\n        try:\n            tags = estimator.__sklearn_tags__()\n        except AttributeError as exc:\n            if str(exc) == \"'super' object has no attribute '__sklearn_tags__'\":\n                # workaround the regression reported in\n                # https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\n                # `__sklearn_tags__` is implemented by calling\n                # `super().__sklearn_tags__()` but there is no `__sklearn_tags__`\n                # method in the base class.\n                warnings.warn(\n                    f\"The following error was raised: {str(exc)}. It seems that \"\n                    \"there are no classes that implement `__sklearn_tags__` \"\n                    \"in the MRO and/or all classes in the MRO call \"\n                    \"`super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Make sure to inherit from \"\n                    \"`BaseEstimator` which implements `__sklearn_tags__` (or \"\n                    \"alternatively define `__sklearn_tags__` but we don't recommend \"\n                    \"this approach). Note that `BaseEstimator` needs to be on the \"\n                    \"right side of other Mixins in the inheritance order. The \"\n                    \"default are now used instead since retrieving tags failed. \"\n                    \"This warning will be replaced by an error in 1.7.\",\n                    category=DeprecationWarning,\n                )\n                tags = default_tags(estimator)\n            else:\n                raise\n    else:\n        # TODO(1.7): Remove this branch of the code\n        # Let's go through the MRO and patch each class implementing _more_tags\n        sklearn_tags_provider = {}\n        more_tags_provider = {}\n        class_order = []\n        for klass in reversed(type(estimator).mro()):\n            if \"__sklearn_tags__\" in vars(klass):\n                sklearn_tags_provider[klass] = klass.__sklearn_tags__(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                class_order.append(klass)\n            elif \"_more_tags\" in vars(klass):\n                more_tags_provider[klass] = klass._more_tags(estimator)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                class_order.append(klass)\n\n        # Find differences between consecutive in the case of __sklearn_tags__\n        # inheritance\n        sklearn_tags_diff = {}\n        items = list(sklearn_tags_provider.items())\n        for current_item, next_item in zip(items[:-1], items[1:]):\n            current_name, current_tags = current_item\n            next_name, next_tags = next_item\n            current_tags = _to_old_tags(current_tags)\n            next_tags = _to_old_tags(next_tags)\n\n            # Compare tags and store differences\n            diff = {}\n            for key in current_tags:\n                if current_tags[key] != next_tags[key]:\n                    diff[key] = next_tags[key]\n\n            sklearn_tags_diff[next_name] = diff\n\n        tags = {}\n        for klass in class_order:\n            if klass in sklearn_tags_diff:\n                tags.update(sklearn_tags_diff[klass])\n            elif klass in more_tags_provider:\n                tags.update(more_tags_provider[klass])\n\n        tags = _to_new_tags(\n            {**_to_old_tags(default_tags(estimator)), **tags}, estimator\n        )\n\n    return tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _safe_tags(estimator, key=None):\n    warnings.warn(\n        \"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed in \"\n        \"1.7. Use the public `get_tags` function instead and make sure to implement \"\n        \"the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\",\n        category=DeprecationWarning,\n    )\n    tags = _to_old_tags(get_tags(estimator))\n\n    if key is not None:\n        if key not in tags:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"The key {key} is not defined for the class \"\n                f\"{estimator.__class__.__name__}.\"\n            )\n        return tags[key]\n    return tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _to_new_tags(old_tags, estimator=None):\n    \"\"\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\"\"\"\n    input_tags = InputTags(\n        one_d_array=\"1darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        two_d_array=\"2darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        three_d_array=\"3darray\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        sparse=\"sparse\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        categorical=\"categorical\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        string=\"string\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        dict=\"dict\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        positive_only=old_tags[\"requires_positive_X\"],\n        allow_nan=old_tags[\"allow_nan\"],\n        pairwise=old_tags[\"pairwise\"],\n    )\n    target_tags = TargetTags(\n        required=old_tags[\"requires_y\"],\n        one_d_labels=\"1dlabels\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        two_d_labels=\"2dlabels\" in old_tags[\"X_types\"],\n        positive_only=old_tags[\"requires_positive_y\"],\n        multi_output=old_tags[\"multioutput\"] or old_tags[\"multioutput_only\"],\n        single_output=not old_tags[\"multioutput_only\"],\n    )\n    if estimator is not None and (\n        hasattr(estimator, \"transform\") or hasattr(estimator, \"fit_transform\")\n    ):\n        transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\n            preserves_dtype=old_tags[\"preserves_dtype\"],\n        )\n    else:\n        transformer_tags = None\n    estimator_type = getattr(estimator, \"_estimator_type\", None)\n    if estimator_type == \"classifier\":\n        classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\n            poor_score=old_tags[\"poor_score\"],\n            multi_class=not old_tags[\"binary_only\"],\n            multi_label=old_tags[\"multilabel\"],\n        )\n    else:\n        classifier_tags = None\n    if estimator_type == \"regressor\":\n        regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\n            poor_score=old_tags[\"poor_score\"],\n        )\n    else:\n        regressor_tags = None\n    return Tags(\n        estimator_type=estimator_type,\n        target_tags=target_tags,\n        transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n        classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n        regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n        input_tags=input_tags,\n        array_api_support=old_tags[\"array_api_support\"],\n        no_validation=old_tags[\"no_validation\"],\n        non_deterministic=old_tags[\"non_deterministic\"],\n        requires_fit=old_tags[\"requires_fit\"],\n        _skip_test=old_tags[\"_skip_test\"],\n    )\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this function\ndef _to_old_tags(new_tags):\n    \"\"\"Utility function convert old tags (dictionary) to new tags (dataclass).\"\"\"\n    if new_tags.classifier_tags:\n        binary_only = not new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_class\n        multilabel = new_tags.classifier_tags.multi_label\n        poor_score_clf = new_tags.classifier_tags.poor_score\n    else:\n        binary_only = False\n        multilabel = False\n        poor_score_clf = False\n\n    if new_tags.regressor_tags:\n        poor_score_reg = new_tags.regressor_tags.poor_score\n    else:\n        poor_score_reg = False\n\n    if new_tags.transformer_tags:\n        preserves_dtype = new_tags.transformer_tags.preserves_dtype\n    else:\n        preserves_dtype = [\"float64\"]\n\n    tags = {\n        \"allow_nan\": new_tags.input_tags.allow_nan,\n        \"array_api_support\": new_tags.array_api_support,\n        \"binary_only\": binary_only,\n        \"multilabel\": multilabel,\n        \"multioutput\": new_tags.target_tags.multi_output,\n        \"multioutput_only\": (\n            not new_tags.target_tags.single_output and new_tags.target_tags.multi_output\n        ),\n        \"no_validation\": new_tags.no_validation,\n        \"non_deterministic\": new_tags.non_deterministic,\n        \"pairwise\": new_tags.input_tags.pairwise,\n        \"preserves_dtype\": preserves_dtype,\n        \"poor_score\": poor_score_clf or poor_score_reg,\n        \"requires_fit\": new_tags.requires_fit,\n        \"requires_positive_X\": new_tags.input_tags.positive_only,\n        \"requires_y\": new_tags.target_tags.required,\n        \"requires_positive_y\": new_tags.target_tags.positive_only,\n        \"_skip_test\": new_tags._skip_test,\n        \"stateless\": new_tags.requires_fit,\n    }\n    X_types = []\n    if new_tags.input_tags.one_d_array:\n        X_types.append(\"1darray\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.two_d_array:\n        X_types.append(\"2darray\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.three_d_array:\n        X_types.append(\"3darray\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.sparse:\n        X_types.append(\"sparse\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.categorical:\n        X_types.append(\"categorical\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.string:\n        X_types.append(\"string\")\n    if new_tags.input_tags.dict:\n        X_types.append(\"dict\")\n    if new_tags.target_tags.one_d_labels:\n        X_types.append(\"1dlabels\")\n    if new_tags.target_tags.two_d_labels:\n        X_types.append(\"2dlabels\")\n    tags[\"X_types\"] = X_types\n    return tags\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/sklearn/utils/_tags.py",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

"{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"Base classes for all estimators and various utility functions.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: \\n3: # Authors: The scikit-learn developers\\n4: # SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause\\n5: \\n6: import copy\\n7: import functools\\n8: import inspect\\n9: import platform\\n10: import re\\n11: import warnings\\n12: from collections import defaultdict\\n13: \\n14: import numpy as np\\n15: \\n16: from . import __version__\\n17: from ._config import config_context, get_config\\n18: from .exceptions import InconsistentVersionWarning\\n19: from .utils._estimator_html_repr import _HTMLDocumentationLinkMixin, estimator_html_repr\\n20: from .utils._metadata_requests import _MetadataRequester, _routing_enabled\\n21: from .utils._param_validation import validate_parameter_constraints\\n22: from .utils._set_output import _SetOutputMixin\\n23: from .utils._tags import (\\n24:     ClassifierTags,\\n25:     RegressorTags,\\n26:     Tags,\\n27:     TargetTags,\\n28:     TransformerTags,\\n29:     get_tags,\\n30: )\\n31: from .utils.fixes import _IS_32BIT\\n32: from .utils.validation import (\\n33:     _check_feature_names,\\n34:     _check_feature_names_in,\\n35:     _check_n_features,\\n36:     _generate_get_feature_names_out,\\n37:     _is_fitted,\\n38:     check_array,\\n39:     check_is_fitted,\\n40:     validate_data,\\n41: )\\n42: \\n43: \\n44: def clone(estimator, *, safe=True):\\n45:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Construct a new unfitted estimator with the same parameters.\\n46: \\n47:     Clone does a deep copy of the model in an estimator\\n48:     without actually copying attached data. It returns a new estimator\\n49:     with the same parameters that has not been fitted on any data.\\n50: \\n51:     .. versionchanged:: 1.3\\n52:         Delegates to `estimator.__sklearn_clone__` if the method exists.\\n53: \\n54:     Parameters\\n55:     ----------\\n56:     estimator : {list, tuple, set} of estimator instance or a single \\\\\\n57:             estimator instance\\n58:         The estimator or group of estimators to be cloned.\\n59:     safe : bool, default=True\\n60:         If safe is False, clone will fall back to a deep copy on objects\\n61:         that are not estimators. Ignored if `estimator.__sklearn_clone__`\\n62:         exists.\\n63: \\n64:     Returns\\n65:     -------\\n66:     estimator : object\\n67:         The deep copy of the input, an estimator if input is an estimator.\\n68: \\n69:     Notes\\n70:     -----\\n71:     If the estimator's `random_state` parameter is an integer (or if the\\n72:     estimator doesn't have a `random_state` parameter), an *exact clone* is\\n73:     returned: the clone and the original estimator will give the exact same\\n74:     results. Otherwise, *statistical clone* is returned: the clone might\\n75:     return different results from the original estimator. More details can be\\n76:     found in :ref:`randomness`.\\n77: \\n78:     Examples\\n79:     --------\\n80:     >>> from sklearn.base import clone\\n81:     >>> from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression\\n82:     >>> X = [[-1, 0], [0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0]]\\n83:     >>> y = [0, 0, 1, 1]\\n84:     >>> classifier = LogisticRegression().fit(X, y)\\n85:     >>> cloned_classifier = clone(classifier)\\n86:     >>> hasattr(classifier, \\\"classes_\\\")\\n87:     True\\n88:     >>> hasattr(cloned_classifier, \\\"classes_\\\")\\n89:     False\\n90:     >>> classifier is cloned_classifier\\n91:     False\\n92:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n93:     if hasattr(estimator, \\\"__sklearn_clone__\\\") and not inspect.isclass(estimator):\\n94:         return estimator.__sklearn_clone__()\\n95:     return _clone_parametrized(estimator, safe=safe)\\n96: \\n97: \\n98: def _clone_parametrized(estimator, *, safe=True):\\n99:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Default implementation of clone. See :func:`sklearn.base.clone` for details.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n100: \\n101:     estimator_type = type(estimator)\\n102:     if estimator_type is dict:\\n103:         return {k: clone(v, safe=safe) for k, v in estimator.items()}\\n104:     elif estimator_type in (list, tuple, set, frozenset):\\n105:         return estimator_type([clone(e, safe=safe) for e in estimator])\\n106:     elif not hasattr(estimator, \\\"get_params\\\") or isinstance(estimator, type):\\n107:         if not safe:\\n108:             return copy.deepcopy(estimator)\\n109:         else:\\n110:             if isinstance(estimator, type):\\n111:                 raise TypeError(\\n112:                     \\\"Cannot clone object. \\\"\\n113:                     + \\\"You should provide an instance of \\\"\\n114:                     + \\\"scikit-learn estimator instead of a class.\\\"\\n115:                 )\\n116:             else:\\n117:                 raise TypeError(\\n118:                     \\\"Cannot clone object '%s' (type %s): \\\"\\n119:                     \\\"it does not seem to be a scikit-learn \\\"\\n120:                     \\\"estimator as it does not implement a \\\"\\n121:                     \\\"'get_params' method.\\\" % (repr(estimator), type(estimator))\\n122:                 )\\n123: \\n124:     klass = estimator.__class__\\n125:     new_object_params = estimator.get_params(deep=False)\\n126:     for name, param in new_object_params.items():\\n127:         new_object_params[name] = clone(param, safe=False)\\n128: \\n129:     new_object = klass(**new_object_params)\\n130:     try:\\n131:         new_object._metadata_request = copy.deepcopy(estimator._metadata_request)\\n132:     except AttributeError:\\n133:         pass\\n134: \\n135:     params_set = new_object.get_params(deep=False)\\n136: \\n137:     # quick sanity check of the parameters of the clone\\n138:     for name in new_object_params:\\n139:         param1 = new_object_params[name]\\n140:         param2 = params_set[name]\\n141:         if param1 is not param2:\\n142:             raise RuntimeError(\\n143:                 \\\"Cannot clone object %s, as the constructor \\\"\\n144:                 \\\"either does not set or modifies parameter %s\\\" % (estimator, name)\\n145:             )\\n146: \\n147:     # _sklearn_output_config is used by `set_output` to configure the output\\n148:     # container of an estimator.\\n149:     if hasattr(estimator, \\\"_sklearn_output_config\\\"):\\n150:         new_object._sklearn_output_config = copy.deepcopy(\\n151:             estimator._sklearn_output_config\\n152:         )\\n153:     return new_object\\n154: \\n155: \\n156: class BaseEstimator(_HTMLDocumentationLinkMixin, _MetadataRequester):\\n157:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Base class for all estimators in scikit-learn.\\n158: \\n159:     Inheriting from this class provides default implementations of:\\n160: \\n161:     - setting and getting parameters used by `GridSearchCV` and friends;\\n162:     - textual and HTML representation displayed in terminals and IDEs;\\n163:     - estimator serialization;\\n164:     - parameters validation;\\n165:     - data validation;\\n166:     - feature names validation.\\n167: \\n168:     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <rolling_your_own_estimator>`.\\n169: \\n170: \\n171:     Notes\\n172:     -----\\n173:     All estimators should specify all the parameters that can be set\\n174:     at the class level in their ``__init__`` as explicit keyword\\n175:     arguments (no ``*args`` or ``**kwargs``).\\n176: \\n177:     Examples\\n178:     --------\\n179:     >>> import numpy as np\\n180:     >>> from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator\\n181:     >>> class MyEstimator(BaseEstimator):\\n182:     ...     def __init__(self, *, param=1):\\n183:     ...         self.param = param\\n184:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n185:     ...         self.is_fitted_ = True\\n186:     ...         return self\\n187:     ...     def predict(self, X):\\n188:     ...         return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\\n189:     >>> estimator = MyEstimator(param=2)\\n190:     >>> estimator.get_params()\\n191:     {'param': 2}\\n192:     >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\\n193:     >>> y = np.array([1, 0, 1])\\n194:     >>> estimator.fit(X, y).predict(X)\\n195:     array([2, 2, 2])\\n196:     >>> estimator.set_params(param=3).fit(X, y).predict(X)\\n197:     array([3, 3, 3])\\n198:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n199: \\n200:     @classmethod\\n201:     def _get_param_names(cls):\\n202:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get parameter names for the estimator\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n203:         # fetch the constructor or the original constructor before\\n204:         # deprecation wrapping if any\\n205:         init = getattr(cls.__init__, \\\"deprecated_original\\\", cls.__init__)\\n206:         if init is object.__init__:\\n207:             # No explicit constructor to introspect\\n208:             return []\\n209: \\n210:         # introspect the constructor arguments to find the model parameters\\n211:         # to represent\\n212:         init_signature = inspect.signature(init)\\n213:         # Consider the constructor parameters excluding 'self'\\n214:         parameters = [\\n215:             p\\n216:             for p in init_signature.parameters.values()\\n217:             if p.name != \\\"self\\\" and p.kind != p.VAR_KEYWORD\\n218:         ]\\n219:         for p in parameters:\\n220:             if p.kind == p.VAR_POSITIONAL:\\n221:                 raise RuntimeError(\\n222:                     \\\"scikit-learn estimators should always \\\"\\n223:                     \\\"specify their parameters in the signature\\\"\\n224:                     \\\" of their __init__ (no varargs).\\\"\\n225:                     \\\" %s with constructor %s doesn't \\\"\\n226:                     \\\" follow this convention.\\\" % (cls, init_signature)\\n227:                 )\\n228:         # Extract and sort argument names excluding 'self'\\n229:         return sorted([p.name for p in parameters])\\n230: \\n231:     def get_params(self, deep=True):\\n232:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n233:         Get parameters for this estimator.\\n234: \\n235:         Parameters\\n236:         ----------\\n237:         deep : bool, default=True\\n238:             If True, will return the parameters for this estimator and\\n239:             contained subobjects that are estimators.\\n240: \\n241:         Returns\\n242:         -------\\n243:         params : dict\\n244:             Parameter names mapped to their values.\\n245:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n246:         out = dict()\\n247:         for key in self._get_param_names():\\n248:             value = getattr(self, key)\\n249:             if deep and hasattr(value, \\\"get_params\\\") and not isinstance(value, type):\\n250:                 deep_items = value.get_params().items()\\n251:                 out.update((key + \\\"__\\\" + k, val) for k, val in deep_items)\\n252:             out[key] = value\\n253:         return out\\n254: \\n255:     def set_params(self, **params):\\n256:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Set the parameters of this estimator.\\n257: \\n258:         The method works on simple estimators as well as on nested objects\\n259:         (such as :class:`~sklearn.pipeline.Pipeline`). The latter have\\n260:         parameters of the form ``<component>__<parameter>`` so that it's\\n261:         possible to update each component of a nested object.\\n262: \\n263:         Parameters\\n264:         ----------\\n265:         **params : dict\\n266:             Estimator parameters.\\n267: \\n268:         Returns\\n269:         -------\\n270:         self : estimator instance\\n271:             Estimator instance.\\n272:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:         if not params:\\n274:             # Simple optimization to gain speed (inspect is slow)\\n275:             return self\\n276:         valid_params = self.get_params(deep=True)\\n277: \\n278:         nested_params = defaultdict(dict)  # grouped by prefix\\n279:         for key, value in params.items():\\n280:             key, delim, sub_key = key.partition(\\\"__\\\")\\n281:             if key not in valid_params:\\n282:                 local_valid_params = self._get_param_names()\\n283:                 raise ValueError(\\n284:                     f\\\"Invalid parameter {key!r} for estimator {self}. \\\"\\n285:                     f\\\"Valid parameters are: {local_valid_params!r}.\\\"\\n286:                 )\\n287: \\n288:             if delim:\\n289:                 nested_params[key][sub_key] = value\\n290:             else:\\n291:                 setattr(self, key, value)\\n292:                 valid_params[key] = value\\n293: \\n294:         for key, sub_params in nested_params.items():\\n295:             valid_params[key].set_params(**sub_params)\\n296: \\n297:         return self\\n298: \\n299:     def __sklearn_clone__(self):\\n300:         return _clone_parametrized(self)\\n301: \\n302:     def __repr__(self, N_CHAR_MAX=700):\\n303:         # N_CHAR_MAX is the (approximate) maximum number of non-blank\\n304:         # characters to render. We pass it as an optional parameter to ease\\n305:         # the tests.\\n306: \\n307:         from .utils._pprint import _EstimatorPrettyPrinter\\n308: \\n309:         N_MAX_ELEMENTS_TO_SHOW = 30  # number of elements to show in sequences\\n310: \\n311:         # use ellipsis for sequences with a lot of elements\\n312:         pp = _EstimatorPrettyPrinter(\\n313:             compact=True,\\n314:             indent=1,\\n315:             indent_at_name=True,\\n316:             n_max_elements_to_show=N_MAX_ELEMENTS_TO_SHOW,\\n317:         )\\n318: \\n319:         repr_ = pp.pformat(self)\\n320: \\n321:         # Use bruteforce ellipsis when there are a lot of non-blank characters\\n322:         n_nonblank = len(\\\"\\\".join(repr_.split()))\\n323:         if n_nonblank > N_CHAR_MAX:\\n324:             lim = N_CHAR_MAX // 2  # apprx number of chars to keep on both ends\\n325:             regex = r\\\"^(\\\\s*\\\\S){%d}\\\" % lim\\n326:             # The regex '^(\\\\s*\\\\S){%d}' % n\\n327:             # matches from the start of the string until the nth non-blank\\n328:             # character:\\n329:             # - ^ matches the start of string\\n330:             # - (pattern){n} matches n repetitions of pattern\\n331:             # - \\\\s*\\\\S matches a non-blank char following zero or more blanks\\n332:             left_lim = re.match(regex, repr_).end()\\n333:             right_lim = re.match(regex, repr_[::-1]).end()\\n334: \\n335:             if \\\"\\\\n\\\" in repr_[left_lim:-right_lim]:\\n336:                 # The left side and right side aren't on the same line.\\n337:                 # To avoid weird cuts, e.g.:\\n338:                 # categoric...ore',\\n339:                 # we need to start the right side with an appropriate newline\\n340:                 # character so that it renders properly as:\\n341:                 # categoric...\\n342:                 # handle_unknown='ignore',\\n343:                 # so we add [^\\\\n]*\\\\n which matches until the next \\\\n\\n344:                 regex += r\\\"[^\\\\n]*\\\\n\\\"\\n345:                 right_lim = re.match(regex, repr_[::-1]).end()\\n346: \\n347:             ellipsis = \\\"...\\\"\\n348:             if left_lim + len(ellipsis) < len(repr_) - right_lim:\\n349:                 # Only add ellipsis if it results in a shorter repr\\n350:                 repr_ = repr_[:left_lim] + \\\"...\\\" + repr_[-right_lim:]\\n351: \\n352:         return repr_\\n353: \\n354:     def __getstate__(self):\\n355:         if getattr(self, \\\"__slots__\\\", None):\\n356:             raise TypeError(\\n357:                 \\\"You cannot use `__slots__` in objects inheriting from \\\"\\n358:                 \\\"`sklearn.base.BaseEstimator`.\\\"\\n359:             )\\n360: \\n361:         try:\\n362:             state = super().__getstate__()\\n363:             if state is None:\\n364:                 # For Python 3.11+, empty instance (no `__slots__`,\\n365:                 # and `__dict__`) will return a state equal to `None`.\\n366:                 state = self.__dict__.copy()\\n367:         except AttributeError:\\n368:             # Python < 3.11\\n369:             state = self.__dict__.copy()\\n370: \\n371:         if type(self).__module__.startswith(\\\"sklearn.\\\"):\\n372:             return dict(state.items(), _sklearn_version=__version__)\\n373:         else:\\n374:             return state\\n375: \\n376:     def __setstate__(self, state):\\n377:         if type(self).__module__.startswith(\\\"sklearn.\\\"):\\n378:             pickle_version = state.pop(\\\"_sklearn_version\\\", \\\"pre-0.18\\\")\\n379:             if pickle_version != __version__:\\n380:                 warnings.warn(\\n381:                     InconsistentVersionWarning(\\n382:                         estimator_name=self.__class__.__name__,\\n383:                         current_sklearn_version=__version__,\\n384:                         original_sklearn_version=pickle_version,\\n385:                     ),\\n386:                 )\\n387:         try:\\n388:             super().__setstate__(state)\\n389:         except AttributeError:\\n390:             self.__dict__.update(state)\\n391: \\n392:     # TODO(1.7): Remove this method\\n393:     def _more_tags(self):\\n394:         \\\"\\\"\\\"This code should never be reached since our `get_tags` will fallback on\\n395:         `__sklearn_tags__` implemented below. We keep it for backward compatibility.\\n396:         It is tested in `test_base_estimator_more_tags` in\\n397:         `sklearn/utils/testing/test_tags.py`.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n398:         from sklearn.utils._tags import _to_old_tags, default_tags\\n399: \\n400:         warnings.warn(\\n401:             \\\"The `_more_tags` method is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed in \\\"\\n402:             \\\"1.7. Please implement the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\\\",\\n403:             category=DeprecationWarning,\\n404:         )\\n405:         return _to_old_tags(default_tags(self))\\n406: \\n407:     # TODO(1.7): Remove this method\\n408:     def _get_tags(self):\\n409:         from sklearn.utils._tags import _to_old_tags, get_tags\\n410: \\n411:         warnings.warn(\\n412:             \\\"The `_get_tags` method is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed in \\\"\\n413:             \\\"1.7. Please implement the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\\\",\\n414:             category=DeprecationWarning,\\n415:         )\\n416: \\n417:         return _to_old_tags(get_tags(self))\\n418: \\n419:     def __sklearn_tags__(self):\\n420:         return Tags(\\n421:             estimator_type=None,\\n422:             target_tags=TargetTags(required=False),\\n423:             transformer_tags=None,\\n424:             regressor_tags=None,\\n425:             classifier_tags=None,\\n426:         )\\n427: \\n428:     def _validate_params(self):\\n429:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Validate types and values of constructor parameters\\n430: \\n431:         The expected type and values must be defined in the `_parameter_constraints`\\n432:         class attribute, which is a dictionary `param_name: list of constraints`. See\\n433:         the docstring of `validate_parameter_constraints` for a description of the\\n434:         accepted constraints.\\n435:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n436:         validate_parameter_constraints(\\n437:             self._parameter_constraints,\\n438:             self.get_params(deep=False),\\n439:             caller_name=self.__class__.__name__,\\n440:         )\\n441: \\n442:     @property\\n443:     def _repr_html_(self):\\n444:         \\\"\\\"\\\"HTML representation of estimator.\\n445: \\n446:         This is redundant with the logic of `_repr_mimebundle_`. The latter\\n447:         should be favorted in the long term, `_repr_html_` is only\\n448:         implemented for consumers who do not interpret `_repr_mimbundle_`.\\n449:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n450:         if get_config()[\\\"display\\\"] != \\\"diagram\\\":\\n451:             raise AttributeError(\\n452:                 \\\"_repr_html_ is only defined when the \\\"\\n453:                 \\\"'display' configuration option is set to \\\"\\n454:                 \\\"'diagram'\\\"\\n455:             )\\n456:         return self._repr_html_inner\\n457: \\n458:     def _repr_html_inner(self):\\n459:         \\\"\\\"\\\"This function is returned by the @property `_repr_html_` to make\\n460:         `hasattr(estimator, \\\"_repr_html_\\\") return `True` or `False` depending\\n461:         on `get_config()[\\\"display\\\"]`.\\n462:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n463:         return estimator_html_repr(self)\\n464: \\n465:     def _repr_mimebundle_(self, **kwargs):\\n466:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Mime bundle used by jupyter kernels to display estimator\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n467:         output = {\\\"text/plain\\\": repr(self)}\\n468:         if get_config()[\\\"display\\\"] == \\\"diagram\\\":\\n469:             output[\\\"text/html\\\"] = estimator_html_repr(self)\\n470:         return output\\n471: \\n472:     # TODO(1.7): Remove this method\\n473:     def _validate_data(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n474:         warnings.warn(\\n475:             \\\"`BaseEstimator._validate_data` is deprecated in 1.6 and will be removed \\\"\\n476:             \\\"in 1.7. Use `sklearn.utils.validation.validate_data` instead. This \\\"\\n477:             \\\"function becomes public and is part of the scikit-learn developer API.\\\",\\n478:             FutureWarning,\\n479:         )\\n480:         return validate_data(self, *args, **kwargs)\\n481: \\n482:     # TODO(1.7): Remove this method\\n483:     def _check_n_features(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n484:         warnings.warn(\\n485:             \\\"`BaseEstimator._check_n_features` is deprecated in 1.6 and will be \\\"\\n486:             \\\"removed in 1.7. Use `sklearn.utils.validation._check_n_features` instead.\\\",\\n487:             FutureWarning,\\n488:         )\\n489:         _check_n_features(self, *args, **kwargs)\\n490: \\n491:     # TODO(1.7): Remove this method\\n492:     def _check_feature_names(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n493:         warnings.warn(\\n494:             \\\"`BaseEstimator._check_feature_names` is deprecated in 1.6 and will be \\\"\\n495:             \\\"removed in 1.7. Use `sklearn.utils.validation._check_feature_names` \\\"\\n496:             \\\"instead.\\\",\\n497:             FutureWarning,\\n498:         )\\n499:         _check_feature_names(self, *args, **kwargs)\\n500: \\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/sklearn/base.py%3FL1-500\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"500: \\n501: \\n502: class ClassifierMixin:\\n503:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Mixin class for all classifiers in scikit-learn.\\n504: \\n505:     This mixin defines the following functionality:\\n506: \\n507:     - set estimator type to `\\\"classifier\\\"` through the `estimator_type` tag;\\n508:     - `score` method that default to :func:`~sklearn.metrics.accuracy_score`.\\n509:     - enforce that `fit` requires `y` to be passed through the `requires_y` tag,\\n510:       which is done by setting the classifier type tag.\\n511: \\n512:     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <rolling_your_own_estimator>`.\\n513: \\n514:     Examples\\n515:     --------\\n516:     >>> import numpy as np\\n517:     >>> from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, ClassifierMixin\\n518:     >>> # Mixin classes should always be on the left-hand side for a correct MRO\\n519:     >>> class MyEstimator(ClassifierMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n520:     ...     def __init__(self, *, param=1):\\n521:     ...         self.param = param\\n522:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n523:     ...         self.is_fitted_ = True\\n524:     ...         return self\\n525:     ...     def predict(self, X):\\n526:     ...         return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\\n527:     >>> estimator = MyEstimator(param=1)\\n528:     >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\\n529:     >>> y = np.array([1, 0, 1])\\n530:     >>> estimator.fit(X, y).predict(X)\\n531:     array([1, 1, 1])\\n532:     >>> estimator.score(X, y)\\n533:     0.66...\\n534:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n535: \\n536:     # TODO(1.8): Remove this attribute\\n537:     _estimator_type = \\\"classifier\\\"\\n538: \\n539:     def __sklearn_tags__(self):\\n540:         tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\\n541:         tags.estimator_type = \\\"classifier\\\"\\n542:         tags.classifier_tags = ClassifierTags()\\n543:         tags.target_tags.required = True\\n544:         return tags\\n545: \\n546:     def score(self, X, y, sample_weight=None):\\n547:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n548:         Return the mean accuracy on the given test data and labels.\\n549: \\n550:         In multi-label classification, this is the subset accuracy\\n551:         which is a harsh metric since you require for each sample that\\n552:         each label set be correctly predicted.\\n553: \\n554:         Parameters\\n555:         ----------\\n556:         X : array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features)\\n557:             Test samples.\\n558: \\n559:         y : array-like of shape (n_samples,) or (n_samples, n_outputs)\\n560:             True labels for `X`.\\n561: \\n562:         sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None\\n563:             Sample weights.\\n564: \\n565:         Returns\\n566:         -------\\n567:         score : float\\n568:             Mean accuracy of ``self.predict(X)`` w.r.t. `y`.\\n569:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n570:         from .metrics import accuracy_score\\n571: \\n572:         return accuracy_score(y, self.predict(X), sample_weight=sample_weight)\\n573: \\n574: \\n575: class RegressorMixin:\\n576:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Mixin class for all regression estimators in scikit-learn.\\n577: \\n578:     This mixin defines the following functionality:\\n579: \\n580:     - set estimator type to `\\\"regressor\\\"` through the `estimator_type` tag;\\n581:     - `score` method that default to :func:`~sklearn.metrics.r2_score`.\\n582:     - enforce that `fit` requires `y` to be passed through the `requires_y` tag,\\n583:       which is done by setting the regressor type tag.\\n584: \\n585:     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <rolling_your_own_estimator>`.\\n586: \\n587:     Examples\\n588:     --------\\n589:     >>> import numpy as np\\n590:     >>> from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, RegressorMixin\\n591:     >>> # Mixin classes should always be on the left-hand side for a correct MRO\\n592:     >>> class MyEstimator(RegressorMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n593:     ...     def __init__(self, *, param=1):\\n594:     ...         self.param = param\\n595:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n596:     ...         self.is_fitted_ = True\\n597:     ...         return self\\n598:     ...     def predict(self, X):\\n599:     ...         return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\\n600:     >>> estimator = MyEstimator(param=0)\\n601:     >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\\n602:     >>> y = np.array([-1, 0, 1])\\n603:     >>> estimator.fit(X, y).predict(X)\\n604:     array([0, 0, 0])\\n605:     >>> estimator.score(X, y)\\n606:     0.0\\n607:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n608: \\n609:     # TODO(1.8): Remove this attribute\\n610:     _estimator_type = \\\"regressor\\\"\\n611: \\n612:     def __sklearn_tags__(self):\\n613:         tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\\n614:         tags.estimator_type = \\\"regressor\\\"\\n615:         tags.regressor_tags = RegressorTags()\\n616:         tags.target_tags.required = True\\n617:         return tags\\n618: \\n619:     def score(self, X, y, sample_weight=None):\\n620:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the coefficient of determination of the prediction.\\n621: \\n622:         The coefficient of determination :math:`R^2` is defined as\\n623:         :math:`(1 - \\\\\\\\frac{u}{v})`, where :math:`u` is the residual\\n624:         sum of squares ``((y_true - y_pred)** 2).sum()`` and :math:`v`\\n625:         is the total sum of squares ``((y_true - y_true.mean()) ** 2).sum()``.\\n626:         The best possible score is 1.0 and it can be negative (because the\\n627:         model can be arbitrarily worse). A constant model that always predicts\\n628:         the expected value of `y`, disregarding the input features, would get\\n629:         a :math:`R^2` score of 0.0.\\n630: \\n631:         Parameters\\n632:         ----------\\n633:         X : array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features)\\n634:             Test samples. For some estimators this may be a precomputed\\n635:             kernel matrix or a list of generic objects instead with shape\\n636:             ``(n_samples, n_samples_fitted)``, where ``n_samples_fitted``\\n637:             is the number of samples used in the fitting for the estimator.\\n638: \\n639:         y : array-like of shape (n_samples,) or (n_samples, n_outputs)\\n640:             True values for `X`.\\n641: \\n642:         sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None\\n643:             Sample weights.\\n644: \\n645:         Returns\\n646:         -------\\n647:         score : float\\n648:             :math:`R^2` of ``self.predict(X)`` w.r.t. `y`.\\n649: \\n650:         Notes\\n651:         -----\\n652:         The :math:`R^2` score used when calling ``score`` on a regressor uses\\n653:         ``multioutput='uniform_average'`` from version 0.23 to keep consistent\\n654:         with default value of :func:`~sklearn.metrics.r2_score`.\\n655:         This influences the ``score`` method of all the multioutput\\n656:         regressors (except for\\n657:         :class:`~sklearn.multioutput.MultiOutputRegressor`).\\n658:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n659: \\n660:         from .metrics import r2_score\\n661: \\n662:         y_pred = self.predict(X)\\n663:         return r2_score(y, y_pred, sample_weight=sample_weight)\\n664: \\n665: \\n666: class ClusterMixin:\\n667:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Mixin class for all cluster estimators in scikit-learn.\\n668: \\n669:     - set estimator type to `\\\"clusterer\\\"` through the `estimator_type` tag;\\n670:     - `fit_predict` method returning the cluster labels associated to each sample.\\n671: \\n672:     Examples\\n673:     --------\\n674:     >>> import numpy as np\\n675:     >>> from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, ClusterMixin\\n676:     >>> class MyClusterer(ClusterMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n677:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n678:     ...         self.labels_ = np.ones(shape=(len(X),), dtype=np.int64)\\n679:     ...         return self\\n680:     >>> X = [[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]]\\n681:     >>> MyClusterer().fit_predict(X)\\n682:     array([1, 1, 1])\\n683:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n684: \\n685:     # TODO(1.8): Remove this attribute\\n686:     _estimator_type = \\\"clusterer\\\"\\n687: \\n688:     def __sklearn_tags__(self):\\n689:         tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\\n690:         tags.estimator_type = \\\"clusterer\\\"\\n691:         if tags.transformer_tags is not None:\\n692:             tags.transformer_tags.preserves_dtype = []\\n693:         return tags\\n694: \\n695:     def fit_predict(self, X, y=None, **kwargs):\\n696:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n697:         Perform clustering on `X` and returns cluster labels.\\n698: \\n699:         Parameters\\n700:         ----------\\n701:         X : array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features)\\n702:             Input data.\\n703: \\n704:         y : Ignored\\n705:             Not used, present for API consistency by convention.\\n706: \\n707:         **kwargs : dict\\n708:             Arguments to be passed to ``fit``.\\n709: \\n710:             .. versionadded:: 1.4\\n711: \\n712:         Returns\\n713:         -------\\n714:         labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=np.int64\\n715:             Cluster labels.\\n716:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n717:         # non-optimized default implementation; override when a better\\n718:         # method is possible for a given clustering algorithm\\n719:         self.fit(X, **kwargs)\\n720:         return self.labels_\\n721: \\n722: \\n723: class BiclusterMixin:\\n724:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Mixin class for all bicluster estimators in scikit-learn.\\n725: \\n726:     This mixin defines the following functionality:\\n727: \\n728:     - `biclusters_` property that returns the row and column indicators;\\n729:     - `get_indices` method that returns the row and column indices of a bicluster;\\n730:     - `get_shape` method that returns the shape of a bicluster;\\n731:     - `get_submatrix` method that returns the submatrix corresponding to a bicluster.\\n732: \\n733:     Examples\\n734:     --------\\n735:     >>> import numpy as np\\n736:     >>> from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, BiclusterMixin\\n737:     >>> class DummyBiClustering(BiclusterMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n738:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n739:     ...         self.rows_ = np.ones(shape=(1, X.shape[0]), dtype=bool)\\n740:     ...         self.columns_ = np.ones(shape=(1, X.shape[1]), dtype=bool)\\n741:     ...         return self\\n742:     >>> X = np.array([[1, 1], [2, 1], [1, 0],\\n743:     ...               [4, 7], [3, 5], [3, 6]])\\n744:     >>> bicluster = DummyBiClustering().fit(X)\\n745:     >>> hasattr(bicluster, \\\"biclusters_\\\")\\n746:     True\\n747:     >>> bicluster.get_indices(0)\\n748:     (array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]), array([0, 1]))\\n749:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n750: \\n751:     @property\\n752:     def biclusters_(self):\\n753:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Convenient way to get row and column indicators together.\\n754: \\n755:         Returns the ``rows_`` and ``columns_`` members.\\n756:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n757:         return self.rows_, self.columns_\\n758: \\n759:     def get_indices(self, i):\\n760:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Row and column indices of the `i`'th bicluster.\\n761: \\n762:         Only works if ``rows_`` and ``columns_`` attributes exist.\\n763: \\n764:         Parameters\\n765:         ----------\\n766:         i : int\\n767:             The index of the cluster.\\n768: \\n769:         Returns\\n770:         -------\\n771:         row_ind : ndarray, dtype=np.intp\\n772:             Indices of rows in the dataset that belong to the bicluster.\\n773:         col_ind : ndarray, dtype=np.intp\\n774:             Indices of columns in the dataset that belong to the bicluster.\\n775:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n776:         rows = self.rows_[i]\\n777:         columns = self.columns_[i]\\n778:         return np.nonzero(rows)[0], np.nonzero(columns)[0]\\n779: \\n780:     def get_shape(self, i):\\n781:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Shape of the `i`'th bicluster.\\n782: \\n783:         Parameters\\n784:         ----------\\n785:         i : int\\n786:             The index of the cluster.\\n787: \\n788:         Returns\\n789:         -------\\n790:         n_rows : int\\n791:             Number of rows in the bicluster.\\n792: \\n793:         n_cols : int\\n794:             Number of columns in the bicluster.\\n795:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n796:         indices = self.get_indices(i)\\n797:         return tuple(len(i) for i in indices)\\n798: \\n799:     def get_submatrix(self, i, data):\\n800:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the submatrix corresponding to bicluster `i`.\\n801: \\n802:         Parameters\\n803:         ----------\\n804:         i : int\\n805:             The index of the cluster.\\n806:         data : array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features)\\n807:             The data.\\n808: \\n809:         Returns\\n810:         -------\\n811:         submatrix : ndarray of shape (n_rows, n_cols)\\n812:             The submatrix corresponding to bicluster `i`.\\n813: \\n814:         Notes\\n815:         -----\\n816:         Works with sparse matrices. Only works if ``rows_`` and\\n817:         ``columns_`` attributes exist.\\n818:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n819: \\n820:         data = check_array(data, accept_sparse=\\\"csr\\\")\\n821:         row_ind, col_ind = self.get_indices(i)\\n822:         return data[row_ind[:, np.newaxis], col_ind]\\n823: \\n824: \\n825: class TransformerMixin(_SetOutputMixin):\\n826:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Mixin class for all transformers in scikit-learn.\\n827: \\n828:     This mixin defines the following functionality:\\n829: \\n830:     - a `fit_transform` method that delegates to `fit` and `transform`;\\n831:     - a `set_output` method to output `X` as a specific container type.\\n832: \\n833:     If :term:`get_feature_names_out` is defined, then :class:`BaseEstimator` will\\n834:     automatically wrap `transform` and `fit_transform` to follow the `set_output`\\n835:     API. See the :ref:`developer_api_set_output` for details.\\n836: \\n837:     :class:`OneToOneFeatureMixin` and\\n838:     :class:`ClassNamePrefixFeaturesOutMixin` are helpful mixins for\\n839:     defining :term:`get_feature_names_out`.\\n840: \\n841:     Examples\\n842:     --------\\n843:     >>> import numpy as np\\n844:     >>> from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin\\n845:     >>> class MyTransformer(TransformerMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n846:     ...     def __init__(self, *, param=1):\\n847:     ...         self.param = param\\n848:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n849:     ...         return self\\n850:     ...     def transform(self, X):\\n851:     ...         return np.full(shape=len(X), fill_value=self.param)\\n852:     >>> transformer = MyTransformer()\\n853:     >>> X = [[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]]\\n854:     >>> transformer.fit_transform(X)\\n855:     array([1, 1, 1])\\n856:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n857: \\n858:     def __sklearn_tags__(self):\\n859:         tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\\n860:         tags.transformer_tags = TransformerTags()\\n861:         return tags\\n862: \\n863:     def fit_transform(self, X, y=None, **fit_params):\\n864:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n865:         Fit to data, then transform it.\\n866: \\n867:         Fits transformer to `X` and `y` with optional parameters `fit_params`\\n868:         and returns a transformed version of `X`.\\n869: \\n870:         Parameters\\n871:         ----------\\n872:         X : array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features)\\n873:             Input samples.\\n874: \\n875:         y :  array-like of shape (n_samples,) or (n_samples, n_outputs), \\\\\\n876:                 default=None\\n877:             Target values (None for unsupervised transformations).\\n878: \\n879:         **fit_params : dict\\n880:             Additional fit parameters.\\n881: \\n882:         Returns\\n883:         -------\\n884:         X_new : ndarray array of shape (n_samples, n_features_new)\\n885:             Transformed array.\\n886:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n887:         # non-optimized default implementation; override when a better\\n888:         # method is possible for a given clustering algorithm\\n889: \\n890:         # we do not route parameters here, since consumers don't route. But\\n891:         # since it's possible for a `transform` method to also consume\\n892:         # metadata, we check if that's the case, and we raise a warning telling\\n893:         # users that they should implement a custom `fit_transform` method\\n894:         # to forward metadata to `transform` as well.\\n895:         #\\n896:         # For that, we calculate routing and check if anything would be routed\\n897:         # to `transform` if we were to route them.\\n898:         if _routing_enabled():\\n899:             transform_params = self.get_metadata_routing().consumes(\\n900:                 method=\\\"transform\\\", params=fit_params.keys()\\n901:             )\\n902:             if transform_params:\\n903:                 warnings.warn(\\n904:                     (\\n905:                         f\\\"This object ({self.__class__.__name__}) has a `transform`\\\"\\n906:                         \\\" method which consumes metadata, but `fit_transform` does not\\\"\\n907:                         \\\" forward metadata to `transform`. Please implement a custom\\\"\\n908:                         \\\" `fit_transform` method to forward metadata to `transform` as\\\"\\n909:                         \\\" well. Alternatively, you can explicitly do\\\"\\n910:                         \\\" `set_transform_request`and set all values to `False` to\\\"\\n911:                         \\\" disable metadata routed to `transform`, if that's an option.\\\"\\n912:                     ),\\n913:                     UserWarning,\\n914:                 )\\n915: \\n916:         if y is None:\\n917:             # fit method of arity 1 (unsupervised transformation)\\n918:             return self.fit(X, **fit_params).transform(X)\\n919:         else:\\n920:             # fit method of arity 2 (supervised transformation)\\n921:             return self.fit(X, y, **fit_params).transform(X)\\n922: \\n923: \\n924: class OneToOneFeatureMixin:\\n925:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Provides `get_feature_names_out` for simple transformers.\\n926: \\n927:     This mixin assumes there's a 1-to-1 correspondence between input features\\n928:     and output features, such as :class:`~sklearn.preprocessing.StandardScaler`.\\n929: \\n930:     Examples\\n931:     --------\\n932:     >>> import numpy as np\\n933:     >>> from sklearn.base import OneToOneFeatureMixin, BaseEstimator\\n934:     >>> class MyEstimator(OneToOneFeatureMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n935:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n936:     ...         self.n_features_in_ = X.shape[1]\\n937:     ...         return self\\n938:     >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])\\n939:     >>> MyEstimator().fit(X).get_feature_names_out()\\n940:     array(['x0', 'x1'], dtype=object)\\n941:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n942: \\n943:     def get_feature_names_out(self, input_features=None):\\n944:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get output feature names for transformation.\\n945: \\n946:         Parameters\\n947:         ----------\\n948:         input_features : array-like of str or None, default=None\\n949:             Input features.\\n950: \\n951:             - If `input_features` is `None`, then `feature_names_in_` is\\n952:               used as feature names in. If `feature_names_in_` is not defined,\\n953:               then the following input feature names are generated:\\n954:               `[\\\"x0\\\", \\\"x1\\\", ..., \\\"x(n_features_in_ - 1)\\\"]`.\\n955:             - If `input_features` is an array-like, then `input_features` must\\n956:               match `feature_names_in_` if `feature_names_in_` is defined.\\n957: \\n958:         Returns\\n959:         -------\\n960:         feature_names_out : ndarray of str objects\\n961:             Same as input features.\\n962:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n963:         # Note that passing attributes=\\\"n_features_in_\\\" forces check_is_fitted\\n964:         # to check if the attribute is present. Otherwise it will pass on\\n965:         # stateless estimators (requires_fit=False)\\n966:         check_is_fitted(self, attributes=\\\"n_features_in_\\\")\\n967:         return _check_feature_names_in(self, input_features)\\n968: \\n969: \\n970: class ClassNamePrefixFeaturesOutMixin:\\n971:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Mixin class for transformers that generate their own names by prefixing.\\n972: \\n973:     This mixin is useful when the transformer needs to generate its own feature\\n974:     names out, such as :class:`~sklearn.decomposition.PCA`. For example, if\\n975:     :class:`~sklearn.decomposition.PCA` outputs 3 features, then the generated feature\\n976:     names out are: `[\\\"pca0\\\", \\\"pca1\\\", \\\"pca2\\\"]`.\\n977: \\n978:     This mixin assumes that a `_n_features_out` attribute is defined when the\\n979:     transformer is fitted. `_n_features_out` is the number of output features\\n980:     that the transformer will return in `transform` of `fit_transform`.\\n981: \\n982:     Examples\\n983:     --------\\n984:     >>> import numpy as np\\n985:     >>> from sklearn.base import ClassNamePrefixFeaturesOutMixin, BaseEstimator\\n986:     >>> class MyEstimator(ClassNamePrefixFeaturesOutMixin, BaseEstimator):\\n987:     ...     def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n988:     ...         self._n_features_out = X.shape[1]\\n989:     ...         return self\\n990:     >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])\\n991:     >>> MyEstimator().fit(X).get_feature_names_out()\\n992:     array(['myestimator0', 'myestimator1'], dtype=object)\\n993:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n994: \\n995:     def get_feature_names_out(self, input_features=None):\\n996:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get output feature names for transformation.\\n997: \\n998:         The feature names out will prefixed by the lowercased class name. For\\n999:         example, if the transformer outputs 3 features, then the feature names\\n1000:         out are: `[\\\"class_name0\\\", \\\"class_name1\\\", \\\"class_name2\\\"]`.\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/sklearn/base.py%3FL500-1000\"}"
32. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input

  "path": "sklearn/base.py",
