  assert old_tags == expected_tags\\n642:     assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\\n643: \\n644: \\n645: # TODO(1.7): Remove this test\\n646: def test_tags_no_sklearn_tags_concrete_implementation():\\n647:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Non-regression test for:\\n648:     https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\\n649: \\n650:     There is no class implementing `__sklearn_tags__` without calling\\n651:     `super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Thus, we raise a warning and request to inherit from\\n652:     `BaseEstimator` that implements `__sklearn_tags__`.\\n653:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n654: \\n655:     class MyEstimator(ClassifierMixin):\\n656:         def __init__(self, *, param=1):\\n657:             self.param = param\\n658: \\n659:         def fit(self, X, y=None):\\n660:             self.is_fitted_ = True\\n661:             return self\\n662: \\n663:         def predict(self, X):\\n664:             return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\\n665: \\n666:     X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\\n667:     y = np.array([1, 0, 1])\\n668: \\n669:     my_pipeline = Pipeline([(\\\"estimator\\\", MyEstimator(param=1))])\\n670:     with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The following error was raised\\\"):\\n671:         my_pipeline.fit(X, y).predict(X)\\n672: \\n673:     # check that we still raise an error if it is not a AttributeError or related to\\n674:     # __sklearn_tags__\\n675:     class MyEstimator2(MyEstimator, BaseEstimator):\\n676:         def __init__(self, *, param=1, error_type=AttributeError):\\n677:             self.param = param\\n678:             self.error_type = error_type\\n679: \\n680:         def __sklearn_tags__(self):\\n681:             super().__sklearn_tags__()\\n682:             raise self.error_type(\\\"test\\\")\\n683: \\n684:     for error_type in (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError):\\n685:         estimator = MyEstimator2(param=1, error_type=error_type)\\n686:         with pytest.raises(error_type):\\n687:             get_tags(estimator)\\n688: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/sklearn/utils/tests/test_tags.py\"}"
43. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "from dataclasses import dataclass, fields\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport pytest\n\nfrom sklearn.base import (\n    BaseEstimator,\n    ClassifierMixin,\n    RegressorMixin,\n    TransformerMixin,\n)\nfrom sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline\nfrom sklearn.utils import (\n    ClassifierTags,\n    InputTags,\n    RegressorTags,\n    Tags,\n    TargetTags,\n    TransformerTags,\n    get_tags,\n)\nfrom sklearn.utils._tags import _safe_tags, _to_new_tags, _to_old_tags, default_tags\nfrom sklearn.utils.estimator_checks import (\n    check_estimator_tags_renamed,\n    check_valid_tag_types,\n)\n\n\nclass NoTagsEstimator:\n    pass\n\n\nclass ClassifierEstimator:\n    # This is to test whether not inheriting from mixins works.\n    _estimator_type = \"classifier\"\n\n\nclass EmptyTransformer(TransformerMixin, BaseEstimator):\n    pass\n\n\nclass EmptyRegressor(RegressorMixin, BaseEstimator):\n    pass\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \"ignore:.*no __sklearn_tags__ attribute.*:DeprecationWarning\"\n)\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"estimator, value\",\n    [\n        [NoTagsEstimator(), False],\n        [ClassifierEstimator(), True],\n        [EmptyTransformer(), False],\n        [EmptyRegressor(), True],\n        [BaseEstimator(), False],\n    ],\n)\ndef test_requires_y(estimator, value):\n    assert get_tags(estimator).target_tags.required == value\n\n\ndef test_no___sklearn_tags__with_more_tags():\n    \"\"\"Test that calling `get_tags` on a class that defines `_more_tags` but not\n    `__sklearn_tags__` raises an error.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    class MoreTagsEstimator(BaseEstimator):\n        def _more_tags(self):\n            return {\\\"requires_y\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    with pytest.raises(\n        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_get_tags`\\\"\n    ):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\\\"MoreTagsEstimator\\\", MoreTagsEstimator())\n\n\ndef test_tag_test_passes_with_inheritance():\n    @dataclass\n    class MyTags(Tags):\n        my_tag: bool = True\n\n    class MyEstimator(BaseEstimator):\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            tags_orig = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n            as_dict = {\n                field.name: getattr(tags_orig, field.name)\n                for field in fields(tags_orig)\n            }\n            tags = MyTags(**as_dict)\n            tags.my_tag = True\n            return tags\n\n    check_valid_tag_types(\\\"MyEstimator\\\", MyEstimator())\n\n\n########################################################################################\n# Test for the deprecation\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this\n########################################################################################\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanOldTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"allow_nan\\\": True}\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanNewTags:\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.input_tags.allow_nan = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanOldNewTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"allow_nan\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.input_tags.allow_nan = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"array_api_support\\\": True}\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags:\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.array_api_support = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"array_api_support\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.array_api_support = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass PredictorOldTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"requires_fit\\\": True}\n\n\nclass PredictorNewTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.requires_fit = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass PredictorOldNewTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"requires_fit\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.requires_fit = True\n        return tags\n\n\ndef test_get_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        if predictor_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\"):\n            with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                tags = get_tags(predictor_cls())\n        else:\n            tags = get_tags(predictor_cls())\n        assert tags.requires_fit\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # one mixin and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n        for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n\n            class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, predictor_cls):\n                pass\n\n            if any(\n                base_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\")\n                for base_cls in (predictor_cls, allow_nan_cls)\n            ):\n                with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                    tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n            else:\n                tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n\n            assert tags.input_tags.allow_nan\n            assert tags.requires_fit\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # two mixins and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags,\n    ]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        for array_api_cls in array_api_classes:\n            for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n\n                class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, array_api_cls, predictor_cls):\n                    pass\n\n                if any(\n                    base_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\")\n                    for base_cls in (predictor_cls, array_api_cls, allow_nan_cls)\n                ):\n                    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                        tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n                else:\n                    tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n\n                assert tags.input_tags.allow_nan\n                assert tags.array_api_support\n                assert tags.requires_fit\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \\\"ignore:.*Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method.*:DeprecationWarning\\\"\n)\ndef test_safe_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated in 1.6\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n            tags = _safe_tags(predictor_cls())\n        assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # one mixin and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n        for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n\n            class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, predictor_cls):\n                pass\n\n            with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                tags = _safe_tags(ChildClass())\n\n            assert tags[\\\"allow_nan\\\"]\n            assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # two mixins and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags,\n    ]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        for array_api_cls in array_api_classes:\n            for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n\n                class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, array_api_cls, predictor_cls):\n                    pass\n\n                with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                    tags = _safe_tags(ChildClass())\n\n                assert tags[\\\"allow_nan\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"array_api_support\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \\\"ignore:.*Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method.*:DeprecationWarning\\\"\n)\ndef test__estimator_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"The `_estimator_tags` method is deprecated in 1.6\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n            tags = predictor_cls()._estimator_tags()\n        assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # one mixin and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n        for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n\n            class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, predictor_cls):\n                pass\n\n            with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                tags = ChildClass()._estimator_tags()\n\n            assert tags[\\\"allow_nan\\\"]\n            assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # two mixins and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags,\n    ]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        for array_api_cls in array_api_classes:\n            for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n\n                class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, array_api_cls, predictor_cls):\n                    pass\n\n                with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                    tags = ChildClass()._estimator_tags()\n\n                assert tags[\\\"allow_nan\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"array_api_support\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n\ndef test_roundtrip_tags():\n    estimator = PredictorNewTags()\n    tags = default_tags(estimator)\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(tags), estimator=estimator) == tags\n\n\ndef test_base_estimator_more_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Test that the `_more_tags` and `_get_tags` methods are equivalent for\n    `BaseEstimator`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n    estimator = BaseEstimator()\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_more_tags` method is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        more_tags = BaseEstimator._more_tags(estimator)\n\n    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_get_tags` method is deprecated\\\"):\n        get_tags = BaseEstimator._get_tags(estimator)\n\n    assert more_tags == get_tags\n\n\ndef test_safe_tags():\n    estimator = PredictorNewTags()\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        tags = _safe_tags(estimator)\n\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        tags_requires_fit = _safe_tags(estimator, key=\\\"requires_fit\\\")\n\n    assert tags_requires_fit == tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    err_msg = \\\"The key unknown_key is not defined\\\"\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=err_msg):\n        with pytest.warns(\n            DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated\\\"\n        ):\n            _safe_tags(estimator, key=\\\"unknown_key\\\")\n\n\ndef test_old_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Set to non-default values and check that we get the expected old tags.\\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    class MyClass:\n        _estimator_type = \\\"regressor\\\"\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = None\n            classifier_tags = None\n            regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\n                poor_score=True,\n            )\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=self._estimator_type,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": False,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": False,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n            \\\"2dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n    class MyClass:\n        _estimator_type = \\\"classifier\\\"\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=False,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = None\n            classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\n                poor_score=True,\n                multi_class=False,\n                multi_label=True,\n            )\n            regressor_tags = None\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=self._estimator_type,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n    class MyClass:\n\n        def fit(self, X, y=None):\n            return self  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def transform(self, X):\n            return X  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=False,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\n                preserves_dtype=[\\\"float64\\\"],\n            )\n            classifier_tags = None\n            regressor_tags = None\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=None,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": False,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": False,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this test\ndef test_tags_no_sklearn_tags_concrete_implementation():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Non-regression test for:\n    https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\n\n    There is no class implementing `__sklearn_tags__` without calling\n    `super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Thus, we raise a warning and request to inherit from\n    `BaseEstimator` that implements `__sklearn_tags__`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    class MyEstimator(ClassifierMixin):\n        def __init__(self, *, param=1):\n            self.param = param\n\n        def fit(self, X, y=None):\n            self.is_fitted_ = True\n            return self\n\n        def predict(self, X):\n            return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\n\n    X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\n    y = np.array([1, 0, 1])\n\n    my_pipeline = Pipeline([(\\\"estimator\\\", MyEstimator(param=1))])\n    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The following error was raised\\\"):\n        my_pipeline.fit(X, y).predict(X)\n\n    # check that we still raise an error if it is not a AttributeError or related to\n    # __sklearn_tags__\n    class MyEstimator2(MyEstimator, BaseEstimator):\n        def __init__(self, *, param=1, error_type=AttributeError):\n            self.param = param\n            self.error_type = error_type\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            super().__sklearn_tags__()\n            raise self.error_type(\\\"test\\\")\n\n    for error_type in (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError):\n        estimator = MyEstimator2(param=1, error_type=error_type)\n        with pytest.raises(error_type):\n            get_tags(estimator)\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/sklearn/utils/tests/test_tags.py"
}

"# We can not use pytest here, because we run\n# build_tools/azure/test_pytest_soft_dependency.sh on these\n# tests to make sure estimator_checks works without pytest.\n\nimport importlib\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport unittest\nimport warnings\nfrom inspect import isgenerator\nfrom numbers import Integral, Real\n\nimport joblib\nimport numpy as np\nimport scipy.sparse as sp\n\nfrom sklearn import config_context, get_config\nfrom sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, ClassifierMixin, OutlierMixin, TransformerMixin\nfrom sklearn.cluster import MiniBatchKMeans\nfrom sklearn.datasets import (\n    load_iris,\n    make_multilabel_classification,\n)\nfrom sklearn.decomposition import PCA\nfrom sklearn.exceptions import (\n    ConvergenceWarning,\n    EstimatorCheckFailedWarning,\n    SkipTestWarning,\n)\nfrom sklearn.linear_model import (\n    LinearRegression,\n    LogisticRegression,\n    MultiTaskElasticNet,\n    SGDClassifier,\n)\nfrom sklearn.mixture import GaussianMixture\nfrom sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsRegressor\nfrom sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\nfrom sklearn.svm import SVC, NuSVC\nfrom sklearn.utils import _array_api, all_estimators, deprecated\nfrom sklearn.utils._param_validation import Interval, StrOptions\nfrom sklearn.utils._test_common.instance_generator import (\n    _construct_instances,\n    _get_expected_failed_checks,\n)\nfrom sklearn.utils._testing import (\n    MinimalClassifier,\n    MinimalRegressor,\n    MinimalTransformer,\n    SkipTest,"

"    msg = \"Repr of .* failed with error\"\n    with raises(AssertionError, match=msg):\n        check_estimator_repr(\"NotRepr\", estimator)\n\n\ndef test_check_estimator_tags_renamed():\n    class BadEstimator1:\n        def _more_tags(self):\n            return None  # pragma: no cover\n\n    class BadEstimator2:\n        def _get_tags(self):\n            return None  # pragma: no cover\n\n    class OkayEstimator:\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            return None  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def _more_tags(self):\n            return None  # pragma: no cover\n\n    msg = \"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_get_tags`\"\n    with raises(TypeError, match=msg):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\"BadEstimator1\", BadEstimator1())\n    with raises(TypeError, match=msg):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\"BadEstimator2\", BadEstimator2())\n\n    # This shouldn't fail since we allow both __sklearn_tags__ and _more_tags\n    # to exist so that third party estimators can easily support multiple sklearn\n    # versions.\n    check_estimator_tags_renamed(\"OkayEstimator\", OkayEstimator())\n\n\ndef test_check_classifier_not_supporting_multiclass():\n    \"\"\"Check that when the estimator has the wrong tags.classifier_tags.multi_class\n    set, the test fails.\"\"\""

  "newString": "    with pytest.raises(\n        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_estimator_tags`\\\"\n    ):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\\\"MoreTagsEstimator\\\", MoreTagsEstimator())",
  "oldString": "    with pytest.raises(\n        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_get_tags`\\\"\n    ):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\\\"MoreTagsEstimator\\\", MoreTagsEstimator())",
  "originalFile": "from dataclasses import dataclass, fields\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport pytest\n\nfrom sklearn.base import (\n    BaseEstimator,\n    ClassifierMixin,\n    RegressorMixin,\n    TransformerMixin,\n)\nfrom sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline\nfrom sklearn.utils import (\n    ClassifierTags,\n    InputTags,\n    RegressorTags,\n    Tags,\n    TargetTags,\n    TransformerTags,\n    get_tags,\n)\nfrom sklearn.utils._tags import _safe_tags, _to_new_tags, _to_old_tags, default_tags\nfrom sklearn.utils.estimator_checks import (\n    check_estimator_tags_renamed,\n    check_valid_tag_types,\n)\n\n\nclass NoTagsEstimator:\n    pass\n\n\nclass ClassifierEstimator:\n    # This is to test whether not inheriting from mixins works.\n    _estimator_type = \"classifier\"\n\n\nclass EmptyTransformer(TransformerMixin, BaseEstimator):\n    pass\n\n\nclass EmptyRegressor(RegressorMixin, BaseEstimator):\n    pass\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \"ignore:.*no __sklearn_tags__ attribute.*:DeprecationWarning\"\n)\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"estimator, value\",\n    [\n        [NoTagsEstimator(), False],\n        [ClassifierEstimator(), True],\n        [EmptyTransformer(), False],\n        [EmptyRegressor(), True],\n        [BaseEstimator(), False],\n    ],\n)\ndef test_requires_y(estimator, value):\n    assert get_tags(estimator).target_tags.required == value\n\n\ndef test_no___sklearn_tags__with_more_tags():\n    \"\"\"Test that calling `get_tags` on a class that defines `_more_tags` but not\n    `__sklearn_tags__` raises an error.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    class MoreTagsEstimator(BaseEstimator):\n        def _more_tags(self):\n            return {\\\"requires_y\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    with pytest.raises(\n        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_get_tags`\\\"\n    ):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\\\"MoreTagsEstimator\\\", MoreTagsEstimator())\n\n\ndef test_tag_test_passes_with_inheritance():\n    @dataclass\n    class MyTags(Tags):\n        my_tag: bool = True\n\n    class MyEstimator(BaseEstimator):\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            tags_orig = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n            as_dict = {\n                field.name: getattr(tags_orig, field.name)\n                for field in fields(tags_orig)\n            }\n            tags = MyTags(**as_dict)\n            tags.my_tag = True\n            return tags\n\n    check_valid_tag_types(\\\"MyEstimator\\\", MyEstimator())\n\n\n########################################################################################\n# Test for the deprecation\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this\n########################################################################################\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanOldTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"allow_nan\\\": True}\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanNewTags:\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.input_tags.allow_nan = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanOldNewTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"allow_nan\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.input_tags.allow_nan = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"array_api_support\\\": True}\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags:\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.array_api_support = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"array_api_support\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.array_api_support = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass PredictorOldTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"requires_fit\\\": True}\n\n\nclass PredictorNewTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.requires_fit = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass PredictorOldNewTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"requires_fit\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.requires_fit = True\n        return tags\n\n\ndef test_get_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        if predictor_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\"):\n            with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                tags = get_tags(predictor_cls())\n        else:\n            tags = get_tags(predictor_cls())\n        assert tags.requires_fit\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # one mixin and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n        for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n\n            class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, predictor_cls):\n                pass\n\n            if any(\n                base_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\")\n                for base_cls in (predictor_cls, allow_nan_cls)\n            ):\n                with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                    tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n            else:\n                tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n\n            assert tags.input_tags.allow_nan\n            assert tags.requires_fit\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # two mixins and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags,\n    ]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        for array_api_cls in array_api_classes:\n            for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n\n                class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, array_api_cls, predictor_cls):\n                    pass\n\n                if any(\n                    base_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\")\n                    for base_cls in (predictor_cls, array_api_cls, allow_nan_cls)\n                ):\n                    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                        tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n                else:\n                    tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n\n                assert tags.input_tags.allow_nan\n                assert tags.array_api_support\n                assert tags.requires_fit\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \\\"ignore:.*Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method.*:DeprecationWarning\\\"\n)\ndef test_safe_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated in 1.6\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, allow_nan_classes:\n        for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n\n            class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, predictor_cls):\n                pass\n\n            with PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags,\n    ]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        for array_api_cls tags[\\\"array_api_support\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \\\"ignore:.*Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method.*:DeprecationWarning\\\"\n)\ndef test__estimator_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"The `_estimator_tags` method is deprecated in 1.6\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n            tags = predictor_cls()._estimator_tags()\n        assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # one mixin and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n ChildClass()._estimator_tags()\n\n                assert tags[\\\"allow_nan\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"array_api_support\\\"]\n                assert tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n\ndef test_roundtrip_tags():\n    estimator = PredictorNewTags()\n    tags = default_tags(estimator)\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(tags), estimator=estimator) == tags\n\n\ndef test_base_estimator_more_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Test that the `_more_tags` and `_get_tags` methods are equivalent for\n    `BaseEstimator`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n    estimator = BaseEstimator()\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_more_tags` method is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        more_tags = BaseEstimator._more_tags(estimator)\n\n    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_get_tags` method is deprecated\\\"):\n        get_tags = BaseEstimator._get_tags(estimator)\n\n    assert more_tags == get_tags\n\n\ndef test_safe_tags():\n    estimator = PredictorNewTags()\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        tags = _safe_tags(estimator)\n\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        tags_requires_fit = _safe_tags(estimator, key=\\\"requires_fit\\\")\n\n    assert tags_requires_fit == tags[\\\"requires_fit\\\"]\n\n    err_msg = \\\"The key unknown_key is not defined\\\"\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=err_msg):\n        with pytest.warns(\n            DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_safe_tags` function is deprecated\\\"\n        ):\n            _safe_tags(estimator, key=\\\"unknown_key\\\")\n\n\ndef test_old_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Set to non-default values and check that we get the expected old tags.\\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    class MyClass:\n        _estimator_type = \\\"regressor\\\"\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = None\n            classifier_tags = None\n            regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\n                poor_score=True,\n            )\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=self._estimator_type,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": False,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": False,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n            \\\"2dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n    class MyClass:\n        _estimator_type = \\\"classifier\\\"\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=False,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = None\n            classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\n                poor_score=True,\n                multi_class=False,\n                multi_label=True,\n            )\n            regressor_tags = None\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=self._estimator_type,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n    class MyClass:\n\n        def fit(self, X, y=None):\n            return self  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def transform(self, X):\n            return X  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=False,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\n                preserves_dtype=[\\\"float64\\\"],\n            )\n            classifier_tags = None\n            regressor_tags = None\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=None,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": False,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": False,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this test\ndef test_tags_no_sklearn_tags_concrete_implementation():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Non-regression test for:\n    https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\n\n    There is no class implementing `__sklearn_tags__` without calling\n    `super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Thus, we raise a warning and request to inherit from\n    `BaseEstimator` that implements `__sklearn_tags__`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    class MyEstimator(ClassifierMixin):\n        def __init__(self, *, param=1):\n            self.param = param\n\n        def fit(self, X, y=None):\n            self.is_fitted_ = True\n            return self\n\n        def predict(self, X):\n            return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\n\n    X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\n    y = np.array([1, 0, 1])\n\n    my_pipeline = Pipeline([(\\\"estimator\\\", MyEstimator(param=1))])\n    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The following error was raised\\\"):\n        my_pipeline.fit(X, y).predict(X)\n\n    # check that we still raise an error if it is not a AttributeError or related to\n    # __sklearn_tags__\n    class MyEstimator2(MyEstimator, BaseEstimator):\n        def __init__(self, *, param=1, error_type=AttributeError):\n            self.param = param\n            self.error_type = error_type\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            super().__sklearn_tags__()\n            raise self.error_type(\\\"test\\\")\n\n    for error_type in (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError):\n        estimator = MyEstimator2(param=1, error_type=error_type)\n        with pytest.raises(error_type):\n            get_tags(estimator)\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "             return {\\\"requires_y\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover",
        " ",
        "     with pytest.raises(",
        "-        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_get_tags`\\\"",
        "+        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_estimator_tags`\\\"",
        "     ):",
        "         check_estimator_tags_renamed(\\\"MoreTagsEstimator\\\", MoreTagsEstimator())",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 7,

      "oldLines": 7,

    }

  "userModified": false

  "oldString": "def test_base_estimator_more_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Test that the `_more_tags` and `_get_tags` methods are equivalent for\n    `BaseEstimator`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n    estimator = BaseEstimator()\n    with pytest.warns(\n        DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_more_tags` method is deprecated\\\"\n    ):\n        more_tags = BaseEstimator._more_tags(estimator)\n\n    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The `_get_tags` method is deprecated\\\"):\n        get_tags = BaseEstimator._get_tags(estimator)\n\n    assert more_tags == get_tags",
  "originalFile": "from dataclasses import dataclass, fields\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport pytest\n\nfrom sklearn.base import (\n    BaseEstimator,\n    ClassifierMixin,\n    RegressorMixin,\n    TransformerMixin,\n)\nfrom sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline\nfrom sklearn.utils import (\n    ClassifierTags,\n    InputTags,\n    RegressorTags,\n    Tags,\n    TargetTags,\n    TransformerTags,\n    get_tags,\n)\nfrom sklearn.utils._tags import _safe_tags, _to_new_tags, _to_old_tags, default_tags\nfrom sklearn.utils.estimator_checks import (\n    check_estimator_tags_renamed,\n    check_valid_tag_types,\n)\n\n\nclass NoTagsEstimator:\n    pass\n\n\nclass ClassifierEstimator:\n    # This is to test whether not inheriting from mixins works.\n    _estimator_type = \"classifier\"\n\n\nclass EmptyTransformer(TransformerMixin, BaseEstimator):\n    pass\n\n\nclass EmptyRegressor(RegressorMixin, BaseEstimator):\n    pass\n\n\n@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(\n    \"ignore:.*no __sklearn_tags__ attribute.*:DeprecationWarning\"\n)\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"estimator, value\",\n    [\n        [NoTagsEstimator(), False],\n        [ClassifierEstimator(), True],\n        [EmptyTransformer(), False],\n        [EmptyRegressor(), True],\n        [BaseEstimator(), False],\n    ],\n)\ndef test_requires_y(estimator, value):\n    assert get_tags(estimator).target_tags.required == value\n\n\ndef test_no___sklearn_tags__with_more_tags():\n    \"\"\"Test that calling `get_tags` on a class that defines `_more_tags` but not\n    `__sklearn_tags__` raises an error.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    class MoreTagsEstimator(BaseEstimator):\n        def _more_tags(self):\n            return {\\\"requires_y\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    with pytest.raises(\n        TypeError, match=\\\"has defined either `_more_tags` or `_estimator_tags`\\\"\n    ):\n        check_estimator_tags_renamed(\\\"MoreTagsEstimator\\\", MoreTagsEstimator())\n\n\ndef test_tag_test_passes_with_inheritance():\n    @dataclass\n    class MyTags(Tags):\n        my_tag: bool = True\n\n    class MyEstimator(BaseEstimator):\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            tags_orig = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n            as_dict = {\n                field.name: getattr(tags_orig, field.name)\n                for field in fields(tags_orig)\n            }\n            tags = MyTags(**as_dict)\n            tags.my_tag = True\n            return tags\n\n    check_valid_tag_types(\\\"MyEstimator\\\", MyEstimator())\n\n\n########################################################################################\n# Test for the deprecation\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this\n########################################################################################\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanOldTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"allow_nan\\\": True}\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanNewTags:\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.input_tags.allow_nan = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinAllowNanOldNewTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"allow_nan\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.input_tags.allow_nan = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"array_api_support\\\": True}\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags:\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.array_api_support = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags:\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"array_api_support\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.array_api_support = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass PredictorOldTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"requires_fit\\\": True}\n\n\nclass PredictorNewTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.requires_fit = True\n        return tags\n\n\nclass PredictorOldNewTags(BaseEstimator):\n    def _more_tags(self):\n        return {\\\"requires_fit\\\": True}  # pragma: no cover\n\n    def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()\n        tags.requires_fit = True\n        return tags\n\n\ndef test_get_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, test_base_estimator_more_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Test that the `_more_tags` and `_get_tags` methods are equivalent for\n    `BaseEstimator`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n    estimator = BaseEstimator()\n key=\\\"unknown_key\\\")\n\n\ndef test_old_tags():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Set to non-default values and check that we get the expected old tags.\\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    class MyClass:\n        _estimator_type = \\\"regressor\\\"\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = None\n            classifier_tags = None\n            regressor_tags = RegressorTags(\n                poor_score=True,\n            )\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=self._estimator_type,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": False,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": False,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n            \\\"2dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n    class MyClass:\n        _estimator_type = \\\"classifier\\\"\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=False,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = None\n            classifier_tags = ClassifierTags(\n                poor_score=True,\n                multi_class=False,\n                multi_label=True,\n            )\n            regressor_tags = None\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=self._estimator_type,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n    class MyClass:\n\n        def fit(self, X, y=None):\n            return self  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def transform(self, X):\n            return X  # pragma: no cover\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            input_tags = InputTags(\n                one_d_array=True,\n                two_d_array=False,\n                three_d_array=True,\n                sparse=True,\n                categorical=True,\n                string=True,\n                dict=True,\n                positive_only=True,\n                allow_nan=True,\n                pairwise=True,\n            )\n            target_tags = TargetTags(\n                required=False,\n                one_d_labels=True,\n                two_d_labels=False,\n                positive_only=True,\n                multi_output=True,\n                single_output=False,\n            )\n            transformer_tags = TransformerTags(\n                preserves_dtype=[\\\"float64\\\"],\n            )\n            classifier_tags = None\n            regressor_tags = None\n            return Tags(\n                estimator_type=None,\n                input_tags=input_tags,\n                target_tags=target_tags,\n                transformer_tags=transformer_tags,\n                classifier_tags=classifier_tags,\n                regressor_tags=regressor_tags,\n            )\n\n    estimator = MyClass()\n    new_tags = get_tags(estimator)\n    old_tags = _to_old_tags(new_tags)\n    expected_tags = {\n        \\\"allow_nan\\\": True,\n        \\\"array_api_support\\\": False,\n        \\\"binary_only\\\": False,\n        \\\"multilabel\\\": False,\n        \\\"multioutput\\\": True,\n        \\\"multioutput_only\\\": True,\n        \\\"no_validation\\\": False,\n        \\\"non_deterministic\\\": False,\n        \\\"pairwise\\\": True,\n        \\\"preserves_dtype\\\": [\\\"float64\\\"],\n        \\\"poor_score\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_fit\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_positive_X\\\": True,\n        \\\"requires_y\\\": False,\n        \\\"requires_positive_y\\\": True,\n        \\\"_skip_test\\\": False,\n        \\\"stateless\\\": True,\n        \\\"X_types\\\": [\n            \\\"1darray\\\",\n            \\\"3darray\\\",\n            \\\"sparse\\\",\n            \\\"categorical\\\",\n            \\\"string\\\",\n            \\\"dict\\\",\n            \\\"1dlabels\\\",\n        ],\n    }\n    assert old_tags == expected_tags\n    assert _to_new_tags(_to_old_tags(new_tags), estimator=estimator) == new_tags\n\n\n# TODO(1.7): Remove this test\ndef test_tags_no_sklearn_tags_concrete_implementation():\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Non-regression test for:\n    https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/30479\n\n    There is no class implementing `__sklearn_tags__` without calling\n    `super().__sklearn_tags__()`. Thus, we raise a warning and request to inherit from\n    `BaseEstimator` that implements `__sklearn_tags__`.\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    class MyEstimator(ClassifierMixin):\n        def __init__(self, *, param=1):\n            self.param = param\n\n        def fit(self, X, y=None):\n            self.is_fitted_ = True\n            return self\n\n        def predict(self, X):\n            return np.full(shape=X.shape[0], fill_value=self.param)\n\n    X = np.array([[1, 2], [2, 3], [3, 4]])\n    y = np.array([1, 0, 1])\n\n    my_pipeline = Pipeline([(\\\"estimator\\\", MyEstimator(param=1))])\n    with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=\\\"The following error was raised\\\"):\n        my_pipeline.fit(X, y).predict(X)\n\n    # check that we still raise an error if it is not a AttributeError or related to\n    # __sklearn_tags__\n    class MyEstimator2(MyEstimator, BaseEstimator):\n        def __init__(self, *, param=1, error_type=AttributeError):\n            self.param = param\n            self.error_type = error_type\n\n        def __sklearn_tags__(self):\n            super().__sklearn_tags__()\n            raise self.error_type(\\\"test\\\")\n\n    for error_type in (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError):\n        estimator = MyEstimator2(param=1, error_type=error_type)\n        with pytest.raises(error_type):\n            get_tags(estimator)\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

  "file": {
    "content": "def test_get_tags_backward_compatibility():\n    warn_msg = \\\"Please define the `__sklearn_tags__` method\\\"\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # only predictor inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        if predictor_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\"):\n            with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                tags = get_tags(predictor_cls())\n        else:\n            tags = get_tags(predictor_cls())\n        assert tags.requires_fit\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # one mixin and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n        for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n\n            class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, predictor_cls):\n                pass\n\n            if any(\n                base_cls.__name__.endswith(\\\"OldTags\\\")\n                for base_cls in (predictor_cls, allow_nan_cls)\n            ):\n                with pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning, match=warn_msg):\n                    tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n            else:\n                tags = get_tags(ChildClass())\n\n            assert tags.input_tags.allow_nan\n            assert tags.requires_fit\n\n    ####################################################################################\n    # two mixins and one predictor all inheriting from BaseEstimator\n    predictor_classes = [PredictorNewTags, PredictorOldNewTags, PredictorOldTags]\n    array_api_classes = [\n        MixinArrayApiSupportNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldNewTags,\n        MixinArrayApiSupportOldTags,\n    ]\n    allow_nan_classes = [\n        MixinAllowNanNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldNewTags,\n        MixinAllowNanOldTags,\n    ]\n\n    for predictor_cls in predictor_classes:\n        for array_api_cls in array_api_classes:\n            for allow_nan_cls in allow_nan_classes:\n\n                class ChildClass(allow_nan_cls, array_api_cls, predictor_cls):",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"775:   :func:`utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks` is now deprecated,\\n776:   and support for classes will be removed in 0.24. Pass instances instead.\\n777:   :pr:`17032` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n778: \\n779: - |API| The private utility `_safe_tags` in `utils.estimator_checks` was\\n780:   removed, hence all tags should be obtained through `estimator._get_tags()`.\\n781:   Note that Mixins like `RegressorMixin` must come *before* base classes\\n782:   in the MRO for `_get_tags()` to work properly.\\n783:   :pr:`16950` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n784: \\n785: - |FIX| `utils.all_estimators` now only returns public estimators.\\n786:   :pr:`15380` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n787: \\n788: Miscellaneous\\n789: .............\\n790: \\n791: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/doc/whats_new/v0.23.rst%3FL775-790\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "775:   :func:`utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks` is now deprecated,\n776:   and support for classes will be removed in 0.24. Pass instances instead.\n777:   :pr:`17032` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\n778: \n779: - |API| The private utility `_safe_tags` in `utils.estimator_checks` was\n780:   removed, hence all tags should be obtained through `estimator._get_tags()`.\n781:   Note that Mixins like `RegressorMixin` must come *before* base classes\n782:   in the MRO for `_get_tags()` to work properly.\n783:   :pr:`16950` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\n784: \n785: - |FIX| `utils.all_estimators` now only returns public estimators.\n786:   :pr:`15380` by `Thomas Fan`_.\n787: \n788: Miscellaneous\n789: .............\n790: \n791: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1@ec6ee443c3c32c29d026be5f2e09d6c3de139ce0/-/blob/doc/whats_new/v0.23.rst%3FL775-790"
  }
}

Let me provide a comprehensive summary of all the modifications made to both files.\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\nI have successfully fetched the documentation files from `github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1` and made the requested modifications. Both files have been written to the workspace directory.\n\n### File 1: `/workspace/doc/whats_new/v0.23.rst`\n\n**Changes made:**\n- **Line 779-782 (originally lines 780-782):** Updated the API deprecation note in the `:mod:`sklearn.utils`` section:\n  - Changed `estimator._get_tags()` to `estimator._estimator_tags()`\n  - Changed `_get_tags()` to `_estimator_tags()` in the note about MRO requirements\n  \n**Context:** The text now reads:\n```\n- |API| The private utility `_safe_tags` in `utils.estimator_checks` was\n  removed, hence all tags should be obtained through `estimator._estimator_tags()`.\n  Note that Mixins like `RegressorMixin` must come *before* base classes\n  in the MRO for `_estimator_tags()` to work properly.\n  :pr:`16950` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\n```\n\n### File 2: `/workspace/doc/whats_new/v1.6.rst`\n\n**Changes made:**\n- **Line 34:** Updated the deprecation notice in the \"Changes impacting many modules\" section:\n  - Changed `_get_tags` to `_estimator_tags` in the list of deprecated methods\n  \n**Context:** The text now reads:\n```\n- |Fix| `_more_tags`, `_estimator_tags`, and `_safe_tags` are now raising a\n  :class:`DeprecationWarning` instead of a :class:`FutureWarning` to only notify\n  developers instead of end-users.\n  By :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>` in :pr:`30573`\n```\n\n### Key Points\n\n- The deprecation warning message has been preserved in v1.6.rst, indicating the change from DeprecationWarning (for developers) to FutureWarning (for end-users)\n- All replacements were made in documentation/changelog sections, not in example code\n- The method references have been updated to reflect the new naming convention: `_get_tags()` \u2192 `_estimator_tags()`\n- Both files are now consistent with the updated API nomenclature",

    }

97. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "doc/whats_new/v0.23.rst",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/scikit-learn--1.6.1"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: .. include:: _contributors.rst\\n2: \\n3: .. currentmodule:: sklearn\\n4: \\n5: .. _release_notes_0_23:\\n6: \\n7: ============\\n8: Version 0.23\\n9: ============\\n10: \\n11: For a short description of the main highlights of the release, please refer to\\n12: :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_release_highlights_plot_release_highlights_0_23_0.py`.\\n13: \\n14: .. include:: changelog_legend.inc\\n15: \\n16: .. _changes_0_23_2:\\n17: \\n18: Version 0.23.2\\n19: ==============\\n20: \\n21: Changed models\\n22: --------------\\n23: \\n24: The following estimators and functions, when fit with the same data and\\n25: parameters, may produce different models from the previous version. This often\\n26: occurs due to changes in the modelling logic (bug fixes or enhancements), or in\\n27: random sampling procedures.\\n28: \\n29: - |Fix| ``inertia_`` attribute of :class:`cluster.KMeans` and\\n30:   :class:`cluster.MiniBatchKMeans`.\\n31: \\n32: Details are listed in the changelog below.\\n33: \\n34: (While we are trying to better inform users by providing this information, we\\n35: cannot assure that this list is complete.)\\n36: \\n37: Changelog\\n38: ---------\\n39: \\n40: :mod:`sklearn.cluster`\\n41: ......................\\n42: \\n43: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.KMeans` where rounding errors could\\n44:   prevent convergence to be declared when `tol=0`. :pr:`17959` by\\n45:   :user:`J\u00e9r\u00e9mie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n46: \\n47: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.KMeans` and\\n48:   :class:`cluster.MiniBatchKMeans` where the reported inertia was incorrectly\\n49:   weighted by the sample weights. :pr:`17848` by\\n50:   :user:`J\u00e9r\u00e9mie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n51: \\n52: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.MeanShift` with `bin_seeding=True`. When\\n53:   the estimated bandwidth is 0, the behavior is equivalent to\\n54:   `bin_seeding=False`.\\n55:   :pr:`17742` by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n56: \\n57: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.AffinityPropagation`, that\\n58:   gives incorrect clusters when the array dtype is float32.\\n59:   :pr:`17995` by :user:`Thomaz Santana  <Wikilicious>` and\\n60:   :user:`Amanda Dsouza <amy12xx>`.\\n61: \\n62: :mod:`sklearn.decomposition`\\n63: ............................\\n64: \\n65: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in\\n66:   :func:`decomposition.MiniBatchDictionaryLearning.partial_fit` which should\\n67:   update the dictionary by iterating only once over a mini-batch.\\n68:   :pr:`17433` by :user:`Chiara Marmo <cmarmo>`.\\n69: \\n70: - |Fix| Avoid overflows on Windows in\\n71:   :func:`decomposition.IncrementalPCA.partial_fit` for large ``batch_size`` and\\n72:   ``n_samples`` values.\\n73:   :pr:`17985` by :user:`Alan Butler <aldee153>` and\\n74:   :user:`Amanda Dsouza <amy12xx>`.\\n75: \\n76: :mod:`sklearn.ensemble`\\n77: .......................\\n78: \\n79: - |Fix| Fixed bug in `ensemble.MultinomialDeviance` where the\\n80:   average of logloss was incorrectly calculated as sum of logloss.\\n81:   :pr:`17694` by :user:`Markus Rempfler <rempfler>` and\\n82:   :user:`Tsutomu Kusanagi <t-kusanagi2>`.\\n83: \\n84: - |Fix| Fixes :class:`ensemble.StackingClassifier` and\\n85:   :class:`ensemble.StackingRegressor` compatibility with estimators that\\n86:   do not define `n_features_in_`. :pr:`17357` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n87: \\n88: :mod:`sklearn.feature_extraction`\\n89: .................................\\n90: \\n91: - |Fix| Fixes bug in :class:`feature_extraction.text.CountVectorizer` where\\n92:   sample order invariance was broken when `max_features` was set and features\\n93:   had the same count. :pr:`18016` by `Thomas Fan`_, `Roman Yurchak`_, and\\n94:   `Joel Nothman`_.\\n95: \\n96: :mod:`sklearn.linear_model`\\n97: ...........................\\n98: \\n99: - |Fix| :func:`linear_model.lars_path` does not overwrite `X` when\\n100:   `X_copy=True` and `Gram='auto'`. :pr:`17914` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n101: \\n102: :mod:`sklearn.manifold`\\n103: .......................\\n104: \\n105: - |Fix| Fixed a bug where :func:`metrics.pairwise_distances` would raise an\\n106:   error if ``metric='seuclidean'`` and ``X`` is not type ``np.float64``.\\n107:   :pr:`15730` by :user:`Forrest Koch <ForrestCKoch>`.\\n108: \\n109: :mod:`sklearn.metrics`\\n110: ......................\\n111: \\n112: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :func:`metrics.mean_squared_error` where the\\n113:   average of multiple RMSE values was incorrectly calculated as the root of the\\n114:   average of multiple MSE values.\\n115:   :pr:`17309` by :user:`Swier Heeres <swierh>`.\\n116: \\n117: :mod:`sklearn.pipeline`\\n118: .......................\\n119: \\n120: - |Fix| :class:`pipeline.FeatureUnion` raises a deprecation warning when\\n121:   `None` is included in `transformer_list`. :pr:`17360` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n122: \\n123: :mod:`sklearn.utils`\\n124: ....................\\n125: \\n126: - |Fix| Fix :func:`utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` so that all test\\n127:   cases support the `binary_only` estimator tag.\\n128:   :pr:`17812` by :user:`Bruno Charron <brcharron>`.\\n129: \\n130: .. _changes_0_23_1:\\n131: \\n132: Version 0.23.1\\n133: ==============\\n134: \\n135: **May 18 2020**\\n136: \\n137: Changelog\\n138: ---------\\n139: \\n140: :mod:`sklearn.cluster`\\n141: ......................\\n142: \\n143: - |Efficiency| :class:`cluster.KMeans` efficiency has been improved for very\\n144:   small datasets. In particular it cannot spawn idle threads any more.\\n145:   :pr:`17210` and :pr:`17235` by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n146: \\n147: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.KMeans` where the sample weights\\n148:   provided by the user were modified in place. :pr:`17204` by\\n149:   :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n150: \\n151: \\n152: Miscellaneous\\n153: .............\\n154: \\n155: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in the `repr` of third-party estimators that use a\\n156:   `**kwargs` parameter in their constructor, when `changed_only` is True\\n157:   which is now the default. :pr:`17205` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n158: \\n159: .. _changes_0_23:\\n160: \\n161: Version 0.23.0\\n162: ==============\\n163: \\n164: **May 12 2020**\\n165: \\n166: \\n167: Enforcing keyword-only arguments\\n168: --------------------------------\\n169: \\n170: In an effort to promote clear and non-ambiguous use of the library, most\\n171: constructor and function parameters are now expected to be passed as keyword\\n172: arguments (i.e. using the `param=value` syntax) instead of positional. To\\n173: ease the transition, a `FutureWarning` is raised if a keyword-only parameter\\n174: is used as positional. In version 1.0 (renaming of 0.25), these parameters\\n175: will be strictly keyword-only, and a `TypeError` will be raised.\\n176: :issue:`15005` by `Joel Nothman`_, `Adrin Jalali`_, `Thomas Fan`_, and\\n177: `Nicolas Hug`_. See `SLEP009\\n178: <https://scikit-learn-enhancement-proposals.readthedocs.io/en/latest/slep009/proposal.html>`_\\n179: for more details.\\n180: \\n181: Changed models\\n182: --------------\\n183: \\n184: The following estimators and functions, when fit with the same data and\\n185: parameters, may produce different models from the previous version. This often\\n186: occurs due to changes in the modelling logic (bug fixes or enhancements), or in\\n187: random sampling procedures.\\n188: \\n189: - |Fix| :class:`ensemble.BaggingClassifier`, :class:`ensemble.BaggingRegressor`,\\n190:   and :class:`ensemble.IsolationForest`.\\n191: - |Fix| :class:`cluster.KMeans` with ``algorithm=\\\"elkan\\\"`` and\\n192:   ``algorithm=\\\"full\\\"``.\\n193: - |Fix| :class:`cluster.Birch`\\n194: - |Fix| `compose.ColumnTransformer.get_feature_names`\\n195: - |Fix| :func:`compose.ColumnTransformer.fit`\\n196: - |Fix| :func:`datasets.make_multilabel_classification`\\n197: - |Fix| :class:`decomposition.PCA` with `n_components='mle'`\\n198: - |Enhancement| :class:`decomposition.NMF` and\\n199:   :func:`decomposition.non_negative_factorization` with float32 dtype input.\\n200: - |Fix| :func:`decomposition.KernelPCA.inverse_transform`\\n201: - |API| :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n202:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor`\\n203: - |Fix| ``estimator_samples_`` in :class:`ensemble.BaggingClassifier`,\\n204:   :class:`ensemble.BaggingRegressor` and :class:`ensemble.IsolationForest`\\n205: - |Fix| :class:`ensemble.StackingClassifier` and\\n206:   :class:`ensemble.StackingRegressor` with `sample_weight`\\n207: - |Fix| :class:`gaussian_process.GaussianProcessRegressor`\\n208: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.RANSACRegressor` with ``sample_weight``.\\n209: -
